Samsung Galaxy F55 5G First Impressions The Samsung Galaxy F series has consistently delivered a solid balance of features and aggressive pricing. Continuing this trend, Samsung introduces the Galaxy F55 5G, boasting a fresh vegan leather finish, enhanced fast charging, and competitive hardware. Here's our initial take on this new release. Design The standout feature of the Galaxy F55 5G is its vegan leather finish, a first for Samsung. While not unique in the Indian market, Samsung's version feels particularly premium. The Apricot Crush color option, with its bright orange leather and stitch-like pattern, is both eye-catching and luxurious. The flat panel design is user-friendly, though the protruding camera modules can cause slight wobbling on flat surfaces. The device is sleek and lightweight, making it comfortable to hold and use. Display Equipped with a 6.7-inch sAMOLED+ display, the Galaxy F55 5G offers a resolution of 2,400 x 1080 pixels, a 120Hz refresh rate, and up to 1,000 nits of peak brightness. Known for its strong displays, Samsung does not disappoint here, delivering vibrant colors and excellent brightness levels. This display enhances the overall viewing experience, setting a high standard for mid-range smartphones. Performance Powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 processor, the Galaxy F55 5G delivers impressive performance for its class. Our initial tests showed smooth handling of daily tasks. The device runs on OneUI 6.1 based on Android 14, providing a seamless user experience. We'll delve deeper into its performance and UI in our full review. Cameras The Galaxy F55 5G features a versatile triple-camera setup, including a 50-megapixel primary sensor with OIS, an 8-megapixel ultra-wide lens, and a 2-megapixel macro shooter. The 50-megapixel front camera promises high-quality selfies and video calls. Initial testing indicates promising camera performance, which we will explore further in our detailed review. Battery and Charging A 5,000mAh battery powers the Galaxy F55 5G, complemented by 45W fast charging support. This is a notable improvement for Samsung's mid-range lineup, allowing for quick charging and prolonged battery life. Early testing suggests the battery performs well, but we'll provide a more comprehensive analysis in our upcoming review. Conclusion The Samsung Galaxy F55 5G stands out with its premium design, strong display, and capable hardware. The vegan leather finish is a notable highlight, and the inclusion of the Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 processor enhances its value-for-money appeal. We look forward to testing the device further to see how it stacks up against competitors. Stay tuned for our full review.
Poco F6 5G: First Impressions and Key Features Unveiled
OnePlus 12 Glacial White will be available in sole 12GB RAM + 256GB inbuilt storage variant

Samsung Galaxy M35 5G With Exynos 1380 SoC, 50-Megapixel Main Camera Launched Samsung has launched the Galaxy M35 5G in Brazil. This latest addition features the powerful Exynos 1380 chipset, a 50-megapixel main camera, and a 6.6-inch full-HD+ Super AMOLED display. The phone is available in Dark Blue, Grey, and Light Blue. Samsung Galaxy M35 5G Price The Samsung Galaxy M35 5G is priced at BRL 2,699 (approximately Rs. 43,400) for the sole 8GB RAM and 256GB storage variant. It's available for purchase via the Samsung Online Store in Brazil. Samsung Galaxy M35 5G Specifications and Features Display and Design The Samsung Galaxy M35 5G features a 6.6-inch full-HD+ (2,340 x 1,080 pixels) Super AMOLED Infinity-O display. It offers a 120Hz refresh rate and up to 1,000 nits of peak brightness. The phone has a side-mounted fingerprint sensor for security and weighs 222 grams, measuring 162.3 x 78.6 x 9.1mm. Performance Powered by the octa-core Exynos 1380 chipset, the Galaxy M35 5G includes a Mali-G68 MP5 GPU. It comes with 8GB of RAM and 256GB of internal storage, expandable up to 1TB via a microSD card. The device runs on Android 14-based One UI 6.1. Camera The smartphone boasts a triple rear camera setup with a 50-megapixel primary sensor, an 8-megapixel ultra-wide lens, and a 2-megapixel macro shooter. For selfies, it has a 13-megapixel front camera. Battery and Charging The Samsung Galaxy M35 5G is equipped with a 6,000mAh battery supporting 25W wired fast charging. Connectivity The handset supports 5G, dual 4G VoLTE, Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.3, GPS, Glonass, Beidou, Galileo, QZSS, and USB Type-C connectivity. For more details, visit the Samsung Online Store and stay tuned for updates on the India launch.

item.title: Privacy
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: http://themobiletimes.com.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymised string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service Privacy Policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognise and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
[{"id":"155f4c41","elType":"container","settings":[],"elements":[{"id":"43590efb","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: http://themobiletimes.com.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymised string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service Privacy Policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognise and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Last updated: April 06, 2024 This Privacy Policy describes Our policies and procedures on the collection, use and disclosure of Your information when You use the Service and tells You about Your privacy rights and how the law protects You. We use Your Personal data to provide and improve the Service. By using the Service, You agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this Privacy Policy.

Interpretation and Definitions

Interpretation

The words of which the initial letter is capitalized have meanings defined under the following conditions. The following definitions shall have the same meaning regardless of whether they appear in singular or in plural.

Definitions

For the purposes of this Privacy Policy:
  • Account means a unique account created for You to access our Service or parts of our Service.
  • Affiliate means an entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party, where "control" means ownership of 50% or more of the shares, equity interest or other securities entitled to vote for election of directors or other managing authority.
  • Company (referred to as either "the Company", "We", "Us" or "Our" in this Agreement) refers to The Mobile Times, 1214 Barkat Nagar Tonk Road Jaipur 302015.
  • Cookies are small files that are placed on Your computer, mobile device or any other device by a website, containing the details of Your browsing history on that website among its many uses.
  • Country refers to: Rajasthan, India
  • Device means any device that can access the Service such as a computer, a cellphone or a digital tablet.
  • Personal Data is any information that relates to an identified or identifiable individual.
  • Service refers to the Website.
  • Service Provider means any natural or legal person who processes the data on behalf of the Company. It refers to third-party companies or individuals employed by the Company to facilitate the Service, to provide the Service on behalf of the Company, to perform services related to the Service or to assist the Company in analyzing how the Service is used.
  • Usage Data refers to data collected automatically, either generated by the use of the Service or from the Service infrastructure itself (for example, the duration of a page visit).
  • Website refers to The Mobile Times, accessible from https://themobiletimes.com/
  • You means the individual accessing or using the Service, or the company, or other legal entity on behalf of which such individual is accessing or using the Service, as applicable.

Collecting and Using Your Personal Data

Types of Data Collected

Personal Data

While using Our Service, We may ask You to provide Us with certain personally identifiable information that can be used to contact or identify You. Personally identifiable information may include, but is not limited to:
  • Email address
  • First name and last name
  • Phone number
  • Usage Data

Usage Data

Usage Data is collected automatically when using the Service. Usage Data may include information such as Your Device's Internet Protocol address (e.g. IP address), browser type, browser version, the pages of our Service that You visit, the time and date of Your visit, the time spent on those pages, unique device identifiers and other diagnostic data. When You access the Service by or through a mobile device, We may collect certain information automatically, including, but not limited to, the type of mobile device You use, Your mobile device unique ID, the IP address of Your mobile device, Your mobile operating system, the type of mobile Internet browser You use, unique device identifiers and other diagnostic data. We may also collect information that Your browser sends whenever You visit our Service or when You access the Service by or through a mobile device.

Tracking Technologies and Cookies

We use Cookies and similar tracking technologies to track the activity on Our Service and store certain information. Tracking technologies used are beacons, tags, and scripts to collect and track information and to improve and analyze Our Service. The technologies We use may include:
  • Cookies or Browser Cookies. A cookie is a small file placed on Your Device. You can instruct Your browser to refuse all Cookies or to indicate when a Cookie is being sent. However, if You do not accept Cookies, You may not be able to use some parts of our Service. Unless you have adjusted Your browser setting so that it will refuse Cookies, our Service may use Cookies.
  • Web Beacons. Certain sections of our Service and our emails may contain small electronic files known as web beacons (also referred to as clear gifs, pixel tags, and single-pixel gifs) that permit the Company, for example, to count users who have visited those pages or opened an email and for other related website statistics (for example, recording the popularity of a certain section and verifying system and server integrity).
Cookies can be "Persistent" or "Session" Cookies. Persistent Cookies remain on Your personal computer or mobile device when You go offline, while Session Cookies are deleted as soon as You close Your web browser. We use both Session and Persistent Cookies for the purposes set out below:
  • Necessary / Essential Cookies Type: Session Cookies Administered by: Us Purpose: These Cookies are essential to provide You with services available through the Website and to enable You to use some of its features. They help to authenticate users and prevent fraudulent use of user accounts. Without these Cookies, the services that You have asked for cannot be provided, and We only use these Cookies to provide You with those services.
  • Cookies Policy / Notice Acceptance Cookies Type: Persistent Cookies Administered by: Us Purpose: These Cookies identify if users have accepted the use of cookies on the Website.
  • Functionality Cookies Type: Persistent Cookies Administered by: Us Purpose: These Cookies allow us to remember choices You make when You use the Website, such as remembering your login details or language preference. The purpose of these Cookies is to provide You with a more personal experience and to avoid You having to re-enter your preferences every time You use the Website.
For more information about the cookies we use and your choices regarding cookies, please visit our Cookies Policy or the Cookies section of our Privacy Policy.

Use of Your Personal Data

The Company may use Personal Data for the following purposes:
  • To provide and maintain our Service, including to monitor the usage of our Service.
  • To manage Your Account: to manage Your registration as a user of the Service. The Personal Data You provide can give You access to different functionalities of the Service that are available to You as a registered user.
  • For the performance of a contract: the development, compliance and undertaking of the purchase contract for the products, items or services You have purchased or of any other contract with Us through the Service.
  • To contact You: To contact You by email, telephone calls, SMS, or other equivalent forms of electronic communication, such as a mobile application's push notifications regarding updates or informative communications related to the functionalities, products or contracted services, including the security updates, when necessary or reasonable for their implementation.
  • To provide You with news, special offers and general information about other goods, services and events which we offer that are similar to those that you have already purchased or enquired about unless You have opted not to receive such information.
  • To manage Your requests: To attend and manage Your requests to Us.
  • For business transfers: We may use Your information to evaluate or conduct a merger, divestiture, restructuring, reorganization, dissolution, or other sale or transfer of some or all of Our assets, whether as a going concern or as part of bankruptcy, liquidation, or similar proceeding, in which Personal Data held by Us about our Service users is among the assets transferred.
  • For other purposes: We may use Your information for other purposes, such as data analysis, identifying usage trends, determining the effectiveness of our promotional campaigns and to evaluate and improve our Service, products, services, marketing and your experience.
We may share Your personal information in the following situations:
  • With Service Providers: We may share Your personal information with Service Providers to monitor and analyze the use of our Service, to contact You.
  • For business transfers: We may share or transfer Your personal information in connection with, or during negotiations of, any merger, sale of Company assets, financing, or acquisition of all or a portion of Our business to another company.
  • With Affiliates: We may share Your information with Our affiliates, in which case we will require those affiliates to honor this Privacy Policy. Affiliates include Our parent company and any other subsidiaries, joint venture partners or other companies that We control or that are under common control with Us.
  • With business partners: We may share Your information with Our business partners to offer You certain products, services or promotions.
  • With other users: when You share personal information or otherwise interact in the public areas with other users, such information may be viewed by all users and may be publicly distributed outside.
  • With Your consent: We may disclose Your personal information for any other purpose with Your consent.

Retention of Your Personal Data

The Company will retain Your Personal Data only for as long as is necessary for the purposes set out in this Privacy Policy. We will retain and use Your Personal Data to the extent necessary to comply with our legal obligations (for example, if we are required to retain your data to comply with applicable laws), resolve disputes, and enforce our legal agreements and policies. The Company will also retain Usage Data for internal analysis purposes. Usage Data is generally retained for a shorter period of time, except when this data is used to strengthen the security or to improve the functionality of Our Service, or We are legally obligated to retain this data for longer time periods.

Transfer of Your Personal Data

Your information, including Personal Data, is processed at the Company's operating offices and in any other places where the parties involved in the processing are located. It means that this information may be transferred to — and maintained on — computers located outside of Your state, province, country or other governmental jurisdiction where the data protection laws may differ than those from Your jurisdiction. Your consent to this Privacy Policy followed by Your submission of such information represents Your agreement to that transfer. The Company will take all steps reasonably necessary to ensure that Your data is treated securely and in accordance with this Privacy Policy and no transfer of Your Personal Data will take place to an organization or a country unless there are adequate controls in place including the security of Your data and other personal information.

Delete Your Personal Data

You have the right to delete or request that We assist in deleting the Personal Data that We have collected about You. Our Service may give You the ability to delete certain information about You from within the Service. You may update, amend, or delete Your information at any time by signing in to Your Account, if you have one, and visiting the account settings section that allows you to manage Your personal information. You may also contact Us to request access to, correct, or delete any personal information that You have provided to Us. Please note, however, that We may need to retain certain information when we have a legal obligation or lawful basis to do so.

Disclosure of Your Personal Data

Business Transactions

If the Company is involved in a merger, acquisition or asset sale, Your Personal Data may be transferred. We will provide notice before Your Personal Data is transferred and becomes subject to a different Privacy Policy.

Law enforcement

Under certain circumstances, the Company may be required to disclose Your Personal Data if required to do so by law or in response to valid requests by public authorities (e.g. a court or a government agency).

Other legal requirements

The Company may disclose Your Personal Data in the good faith belief that such action is necessary to:
  • Comply with a legal obligation
  • Protect and defend the rights or property of the Company
  • Prevent or investigate possible wrongdoing in connection with the Service
  • Protect the personal safety of Users of the Service or the public
  • Protect against legal liability

Security of Your Personal Data

The security of Your Personal Data is important to Us, but remember that no method of transmission over the Internet, or method of electronic storage is 100% secure. While We strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect Your Personal Data, We cannot guarantee its absolute security.

Children's Privacy

Our Service does not address anyone under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from anyone under the age of 13. If You are a parent or guardian and You are aware that Your child has provided Us with Personal Data, please contact Us. If We become aware that We have collected Personal Data from anyone under the age of 13 without verification of parental consent, We take steps to remove that information from Our servers. If We need to rely on consent as a legal basis for processing Your information and Your country requires consent from a parent, We may require Your parent's consent before We collect and use that information.

Links to Other Websites

Our Service may contain links to other websites that are not operated by Us. If You click on a third party link, You will be directed to that third party's site. We strongly advise You to review the Privacy Policy of every site You visit. We have no control over and assume no responsibility for the content, privacy policies or practices of any third party sites or services.

Changes to this Privacy Policy

We may update Our Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify You of any changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page. We will let You know via email and/or a prominent notice on Our Service, prior to the change becoming effective and update the "Last updated" date at the top of this Privacy Policy. You are advised to review this Privacy Policy periodically for any changes. Changes to this Privacy Policy are effective when they are posted on this page.

Contact Us

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, You can contact us:
  • By email: themobiletimes@gmail.com
  • By visiting this page on our website: https://themobiletimes.com/contact-us/

item.title: Contact Us
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
The Mobile Times> News

Connect with us:

Facebook Twitter Youtube Instagram Linkedin Blogger-b

Mail address

themobiletimes@gmail.com

Office Location

1214 Barkat Nagar, Tonk Road, Jaipur, Rajasthan - 302015

Phone Number

+91-9314001214+91-9829013005

About Us

The Mobile Times is India’s first monthly newsletter on telecom trade, It has been the leading voice in tech news for over 15 years covering Smartphones, Accessories, Gadgets, Technology and more.

More Than 15+ Years We Provide True News​

Media Marketing

Boost your brand with expert media strategies.

Video Marketing

Transform your business with impactful video content.
  • Tailored campaigns for maximum reach
  • Engaging content to captivate your audience
  • High-quality production for all platforms
  • Creative storytelling to enhance your brand
Contact Us

Magazine Subscription

Magazine Subscription

Apply For Advertisment

item.title: Terms and Conditions
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Terms and Conditions for The Mobile Times

Welcome to The Mobile Times, accessible at https://themobiletimes.com/. We are dedicated to providing our visitors with insightful blogs and daily news around the telecom industry, alongside our monthly e-magazine. These Terms and Conditions govern your access to and use of The Mobile Times and its content. By accessing or using the Website, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you disagree with any part of the terms, then you may not access the Website.

  1. Accounts and Membership
    Users are not required to create an account to access The Mobile Times content. Consequently, account creation is not available on our platform.
  2. Content on The Mobile Times
    The content provided on The Mobile Times, including text and images, is sourced from various places on the Internet. While we strive to credit original creators where possible, we do not claim the content to be our own and acknowledge that it remains the property of its respective owners.
    Users may not create, upload, or share content on the Website. The only user-generated content allowed is comments on the blogs or news posts available on our site.
  3. Subscriptions
    The Mobile Times offers free subscriptions to our blogs. By subscribing, users agree to receive updates and newsletters from The Mobile Times.
  4. Promotions
    From time to time, The Mobile Times may host promotions on the website. These are subject to additional terms and conditions that will be made available with each promotion.
  5. Feedback and Suggestions
    We welcome feedback and suggestions from our users. By providing feedback, suggestions, or any related materials to The Mobile Times, you agree that we are free to use, implement, and distribute these without any obligation to provide compensation or credit to you.
  6.  Intellectual Property Rights
    All rights in the original content, features, and functionality of The Mobile Times are owned by the business, its licensors, or other providers of such material and are protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property or proprietary rights laws.
  7. Contact Us
    For any questions regarding these Terms & Conditions, please contact us at themobiletimes@gmail.com or through our contact page athttps://themobiletimes.com/contact-us

item.title: CMF Phone 1 to Feature Dimensity 7300 SoC: What You Need to Know
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Nothing is set to launch the CMF Phone 1, with new rumors suggesting it will feature the Dimensity 7300 SoC, rivaling the Snapdragon 778G+.

CMF Phone 1 Key Specifications

  • Display: 6.67-inch 120Hz OLED
  • Processor: Dimensity 7300 SoC
  • Front Camera: 16MP
  • Rear Cameras: Dual setup with 50MP primary sensor
  • RAM: 8GB LPDDR4x
  • Storage: 128GB, 256GB UFS 2.2 (expandable via microSD)
  • Battery Capacity: 5000 mAh with 33W fast charging
  • Expected Price: USD 249 (Rs. 20,785 approx.)
  • Launch Date: July 2024

Concept and Design

Visual artist Rahul Janardhanan has created a concept image of the CMF Phone 1, showcasing a replaceable plastic back cover and a unique Nothing Lock for exclusive add-ons.

Rumored Features

  • Performance: The Dimensity 7300 SoC, recently benchmarked in the OPPO Reno12 Pro, scores 1043 (single-core) and 2944 (multi-core) on Geekbench 6, similar to the Snapdragon 778G+.
  • Camera: Dual-camera setup with a 50MP primary sensor for high-quality photos.
  • Battery: 5000 mAh battery with 33W fast charging, slightly less powerful than the 45W in the Nothing Phone (2a).

Analysis

The CMF Phone 1 aims to deliver a balance of performance and affordability. With the Dimensity 7300 SoC, users can expect robust performance comparable to higher-end models. The competitive pricing at USD 249 makes it an attractive option for budget-conscious consumers looking for premium features.

Stay tuned for more updates on the CMF Phone 1 as we approach its expected launch date in July 2024.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: CMF Phone 1: What You Need to Know #separator_sa #site_title
New rumors suggest CMF Phone 1 may feature the Dimensity 7300 SoC, Launch expected in July 2024.

item.title: Vivo X Fold 3 Pro : A Strong Foldable Contender
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
With its X Fold 3 Pro, Vivo becomes the fourth brand after Samsung, Tecno, and OnePlus to introduce a book-style foldable in India.

HIGHLIGHTS:

  • The Vivo X Fold 3 Pro is Vivo’s first foldable device in India
  • It packs in some segment-leading hardware specifications
  • The foldable also features an IPX8 rating and wireless charging

Overview

Vivo has come a long way in the Indian market since its debut with the Vivo X1 in 2012. The brand has now moved into the ultra-premium segment with the launch of its first foldable device, the X Fold 3 Pro. This latest offering is designed to compete with the likes of OnePlus Open and Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5. Priced at Rs. 1,59,999, the X Fold 3 Pro is Vivo's most premium offering, demanding high expectations.

Design and Build

The X Fold 3 Pro features a new hinge design that supports the display with minimal creasing, allowing both halves to lay flat when open. It measures 5.2mm when unfolded and 11.2mm when folded, weighing 236 grams. Despite its size, it feels comfortable to hold, with a curved edge cover display and a plastic fibre rear panel.

Display

The device boasts a massive 8.03-inch folding AMOLED panel with a 4:3.55 aspect ratio, making it ideal for media consumption. The 6.53-inch AMOLED cover display has a 21.1:9 aspect ratio, offering a balanced viewing experience.

Hardware and Performance

Powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 SoC, the X Fold 3 Pro includes 16GB of RAM and 512GB of storage. It features a 5,700mAh battery with 120W wired and 50W wireless charging capabilities. The foldable also supports dual nano SIM cards.

Camera

Vivo continues its focus on camera performance with Zeiss branding and T-star coated lenses. The device includes a 50MP primary camera with OIS, a 50MP ultrawide camera, and a 64MP 3X telephoto camera with OIS. Selfies are managed by two 32MP fixed focus cameras.

Software

The X Fold 3 Pro runs on Android 14-based Funtouch OS 14, featuring generative AI integrations in the notes and recording apps, and language translation. Vivo has also incorporated AI to enhance the phone's folding abilities.

Comparison with Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 and OnePlus Open

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5, known for its sturdy build and feature-rich experience, offers a thinner profile when folded (13.4mm) compared to Vivo's 11.2mm. It weighs slightly more at 253 grams. Samsung's cover display is narrower at a 23.1:9 aspect ratio, which some users find less comfortable for typing. However, Samsung provides a similar IPX8 water resistance rating and lacks the 50W wireless charging offered by Vivo.

OnePlus Open: The OnePlus Open stands out with its unique design, featuring a faux leather rear panel and camera-focused approach. Its nearly square 7.8-inch inner display (1:1.1 aspect ratio) isn’t as media-friendly as Vivo's more rectangular 4:3.55 aspect ratio. OnePlus's cover display, at a 20:9 aspect ratio, is wider than Vivo's, offering a different ergonomic experience. However, OnePlus doesn't match Vivo's wireless charging capabilities or battery capacity.

Conclusion

The Vivo X Fold 3 Pro emerges as a solid contender in the foldable market, offering segment-leading hardware features. While Samsung faces competition from Huawei, Vivo's robust specs and innovative design may position it as a strong rival. Stay tuned for our detailed review to see how it performs in daily use.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Vivo X Fold 3 Pro First Impressions: The Perfect Foldable
Samsung is gearing up for the Galaxy S24 FE launch this summer, with early mass production hints suggesting a potential release between June and August.a

item.title: Infinix GT 20 Pro for Mobile Gamers under 25000?
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
If you're on a budget, should you spend more than Rs. 25,000 on a gaming phone?

HIGHLIGHTS:

  • Infinix GT 20 Pro sports a 6.78-inch AMOLED screen
  • The handset features dual speakers tuned by JBL
  • The Infinix GT 20 Pro is equipped with a 5,000mAh battery

Overview

Infinix has launched the GT 20 Pro, a budget-friendly gaming smartphone featuring a MediaTek Dimensity 8200 Ultimate SoC and a dedicated Pixelworks chip for enhanced gaming performance. Priced starting at Rs. 24,999, this device aims to deliver high-end gaming capabilities without breaking the bank. Let's see how it performs.

Design and Build

The GT 20 Pro features Infinix's Cyber Mecha design with a Mecha Loop Lighting system inspired by Nothing's Glyph interface. It has a clear panel on the back with a metallic finish and RGB LED lighting in a 'G' shape for notifications, charging, and music. The phone's sides are made of metallic-looking plastic, providing a secure grip. The power button and volume rocker are on the right, while the SIM tray, USB Type-C port, and speaker grille are at the bottom. There's no 3.5mm headphone jack, but an IR blaster is present for controlling devices.

Display

The phone boasts a 6.78-inch full-HD+ AMOLED screen with a 144Hz refresh rate and 1,300 nits of peak brightness. It offers good visibility outdoors and performs well in everyday apps and games.

Hardware and Performance

Powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 8200 Ultimate chip, the GT 20 Pro includes up to 12GB of LPDDR5X RAM. It features a 5,000mAh battery with 45W fast charging and support for bypass charging while gaming. The phone runs on Android 14 with Infinix's XOS 14 for GT skin, promising two major Android upgrades and a third year of security updates. Connectivity options include 5G, Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth, NFC, and more.

Performance

The GT 20 Pro excels in gaming, supporting up to 90fps in games like BGMI and Call of Duty: Mobile. It handles heavy titles like Genshin Impact on medium settings without stuttering. The phone's cooling fan helps maintain performance during long gaming sessions. Benchmark scores are impressive, with 895,790 points on AnTuTu v10 and strong results on Geekbench and graphics tests.

Camera

The GT 20 Pro features a 108MP primary camera with OIS, a 2MP macro camera, and a 2MP depth sensor. The primary camera captures detailed images in good lighting but struggles with color accuracy in low light. The macro camera is mediocre, and the selfie camera performs decently. The phone supports 4K/60fps video recording with OIS.

Comparison with Competitors

Poco X6 Pro: The Poco X6 Pro outperforms the GT 20 Pro in benchmark tests but falls short in display and gaming-specific features.

Samsung Galaxy F55: The Galaxy F55 offers a better all-around experience, especially in camera performance, but may not match the GT 20 Pro's gaming prowess.

Motorola Edge 50 Fusion and OnePlus Nord CE 4: These phones provide balanced performance with better software experiences but lack the GT 20 Pro's gaming optimization.

Verdict

The Infinix GT 20 Pro is an excellent choice for gamers on a budget, offering top-tier performance, a smooth display, and a clutter-free OS. While other phones in the same price range might offer better overall experiences, the GT 20 Pro stands out for its gaming capabilities.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Infinix GT 20 Pro Review: Power-Packed Gaming on a Budget
Discover Infinix GT 20 Pro Review with a MediaTek Dimensity 8200 Ultimate SoC, AMOLED screen, and 5,000mAh battery. Best gaming phone under Rs. 25,000?

item.title: Samsung Galaxy F55 5G's is Premium at Affordable Prices!
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Samsung Galaxy F55 5G First Impressions

The Samsung Galaxy F series has consistently delivered a solid balance of features and aggressive pricing. Continuing this trend, Samsung introduces the Galaxy F55 5G, boasting a fresh vegan leather finish, enhanced fast charging, and competitive hardware. Here's our initial take on this new release.

Design

The standout feature of the Galaxy F55 5G is its vegan leather finish, a first for Samsung. While not unique in the Indian market, Samsung's version feels particularly premium. The Apricot Crush color option, with its bright orange leather and stitch-like pattern, is both eye-catching and luxurious. The flat panel design is user-friendly, though the protruding camera modules can cause slight wobbling on flat surfaces. The device is sleek and lightweight, making it comfortable to hold and use.

Display

Equipped with a 6.7-inch sAMOLED+ display, the Galaxy F55 5G offers a resolution of 2,400 x 1080 pixels, a 120Hz refresh rate, and up to 1,000 nits of peak brightness. Known for its strong displays, Samsung does not disappoint here, delivering vibrant colors and excellent brightness levels. This display enhances the overall viewing experience, setting a high standard for mid-range smartphones.

Performance

Powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 processor, the Galaxy F55 5G delivers impressive performance for its class. Our initial tests showed smooth handling of daily tasks. The device runs on OneUI 6.1 based on Android 14, providing a seamless user experience. We'll delve deeper into its performance and UI in our full review.

Cameras

The Galaxy F55 5G features a versatile triple-camera setup, including a 50-megapixel primary sensor with OIS, an 8-megapixel ultra-wide lens, and a 2-megapixel macro shooter. The 50-megapixel front camera promises high-quality selfies and video calls. Initial testing indicates promising camera performance, which we will explore further in our detailed review.

Battery and Charging

A 5,000mAh battery powers the Galaxy F55 5G, complemented by 45W fast charging support. This is a notable improvement for Samsung's mid-range lineup, allowing for quick charging and prolonged battery life. Early testing suggests the battery performs well, but we'll provide a more comprehensive analysis in our upcoming review.

Conclusion

The Samsung Galaxy F55 5G stands out with its premium design, strong display, and capable hardware. The vegan leather finish is a notable highlight, and the inclusion of the Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 processor enhances its value-for-money appeal. We look forward to testing the device further to see how it stacks up against competitors. Stay tuned for our full review.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Samsung Galaxy F55 at Affordable Prices #separator_sa #site_title
Discover the Samsung Galaxy F55 5G with its vegan leather finish, Snapdragon 7 Gen 1 processor, and 45W fast charging. Read our first impressions for more.

item.title: Poco F6 5G : All You Need To Know!
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
The Poco F6 5G has officially launched in India, making waves as the first device in the country to feature Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 SoC. This new entrant in the competitive mid-range segment promises a lot of value for its price, reminiscent of the well-received Poco X6 Pro. After spending a few days with the device, here are our initial thoughts.

Design

The Poco F6 5G sports a polycarbonate rear panel with a matte finish, which is resistant to fingerprints. The design includes a plastic frame and a dual-camera setup, featuring two large camera rings and a flash circle on the top left corner. Unlike its predecessor, the Poco F5, this model opts for a dual-camera setup instead of a triple camera. While it lacks a 3.5mm headphone port, it retains features like the IR blaster. The rear panel is a bit slippery, but the overall build is ergonomic and easy to grip.

The front showcases a 6.67-inch AMOLED LTPS panel with 1.5K resolution, a 120Hz refresh rate, and a 94.7% screen-to-body ratio. The display is protected by Corning Gorilla Glass Victus and offers an IP64 rating for water and dust resistance. The slightly thicker chin and thin bezels enhance the viewing experience, making the phone feel premium despite its competitive pricing.

Performance

The highlight of the Poco F6 5G is its Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 chipset, a powerful processor that borrows its architecture from the flagship Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 SoC. During our brief testing, the performance was exceptional, handling daily tasks and gaming with ease. The device comes with LPDDR5X RAM and UFS 4.0 storage, available in two variants: 8GB RAM with 256GB storage and 12GB RAM with 512GB storage, priced at Rs. 29,999 and Rs. 31,999, respectively.

Cameras

Poco has upgraded the camera setup, featuring a 50-megapixel Sony IMX882 primary sensor with an f/1.6 aperture and OIS, and an 8-megapixel Sony IMX335 ultra-wide-angle lens with a 119-degree field of view. The main camera’s wide aperture is a standout, promising better low-light performance. For selfies, there’s a 20-megapixel front camera. Initial impressions suggest a significant improvement over previous models, but further testing will reveal the full potential of these sensors.

Battery and Software

The device houses a 5,000mAh battery with 90W fast charging support, and a 120W fast charger is included in the box. This is a noteworthy feature, ensuring rapid charging times. The Poco F6 5G runs on Xiaomi's new HyperOS based on Android 14, offering several AI features like AI Image Expansion, AI Eraser Pro, and AI Bokeh, enhancing the overall user experience.

Conclusion

The Poco F6 5G is positioned as a strong contender in the mid-range market, offering a robust Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 processor, an impressive camera setup, and fast charging capabilities. While our initial impressions are positive, we recommend waiting for our full review to see how it stacks up against the competition in various parameters. Stay tuned for a comprehensive analysis!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Poco F6 5G : All You Need To Know #separator_sa #site_title
Discover the new Poco F6 5G with Snapdragon 8s Gen 3, 50MP camera, and 90W fast charging. Read our first impressions and features overview.

item.title: OnePlus 12 Glacial White Launched in India at Exciting Price?
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
OnePlus 12 Glacial White Colour Option Launched

The OnePlus 12 in Glacial White has officially launched in India. Teased last week, this variant is now listed on the OnePlus India website. Initially released in January, the OnePlus 12 was available in Flowy Emerald and Silky Black. The Glacial White variant carries the same high-end specifications as the original models.

OnePlus 12 Glacial White Price and Availability

The new OnePlus 12 Glacial White comes with 12GB RAM and 256GB storage, priced at Rs. 64,999. The smartphone will be available on Amazon, the OnePlus website, OnePlus Experience stores, and official partner stores across India starting June 6.

OnePlus 12 Glacial White Specifications

The OnePlus 12 Glacial White features a 6.82-inch quad-HD+ LTPO 4.0 AMOLED display with a resolution of 1,440 x 3,168 pixels and an adaptive refresh rate between 1Hz and 120Hz. The screen also boasts a peak brightness of 4,500 nits.

Powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 SoC, the device includes 12GB of LPDDR5x RAM and 256GB of UFS 4.0 storage. Running on Android 14-based OxygenOS 14, it offers a seamless user experience.

For photography, the OnePlus 12 Glacial White sports a triple rear camera setup. It includes a 50-megapixel primary shooter with a Sony LYT-808 sensor, a 48-megapixel ultra-wide-angle lens, and a 64-megapixel periscope telephoto lens with 3x optical zoom, all tuned by Hasselblad. The front camera is a 32-megapixel sensor, perfect for selfies.

In terms of connectivity, the device supports 5G, 4G LTE, Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 5.4, GPS, and NFC. The smartphone is equipped with a 5,400mAh battery, 100W SuperVOOC wired charging, 50W wireless charging, and 10W reverse wireless charging capabilities.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: OnePlus 12 Glacial White Launched in India#separator_sa #site_title
The OnePlus 12 Glacial White is now available in India for Rs. 64,999. Check out its price, specifications, and availability.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy M35 5G Launched: Everything you need to know!
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Samsung has introduced the Galaxy M35 5G, featuring the Exynos 1380 chipset and a 50MP triple camera setup.

Key Highlights

  • Display: 6.6-inch full-HD+ Super AMOLED screen with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Camera: 50MP main sensor, 8MP ultra-wide, 2MP macro, 13MP front camera
  • Processor: Octa-core Exynos 1380 chipset
  • Battery: 6,000mAh with 25W fast charging
  • Security: Side-mounted fingerprint sensor
  • OS: Android 14-based One UI 6.1

The Samsung Galaxy M35 5G boasts a vibrant 6.6-inch Super AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate and 1,000 nits peak brightness. It's powered by an Exynos 1380 SoC, paired with 8GB RAM and 256GB storage, expandable up to 1TB via microSD.

The device sports a triple camera system, featuring a 50MP primary sensor, an 8MP ultra-wide lens, and a 2MP macro shooter. For selfies, it has a 13MP front camera. The phone supports 5G connectivity, dual 4G VoLTE, Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.3, and USB Type-C.

Pricing and Availability

The Samsung Galaxy M35 5G is priced at BRL 2,699 (approx. Rs. 43,400) for the 8GB + 256GB variant. It is available in Dark Blue, Grey, and Light Blue through the Samsung Online Store in Brazil.

Additional Features

  • Battery: 6,000mAh with 25W fast charging
  • Dimensions: 162.3 x 78.6 x 9.1mm
  • Weight: 222g
  • Sound: Dolby Atmos support

Stay tuned for updates on its launch in other markets, including India.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Samsung Galaxy M35 5G launched with Exynos 1380 SoC, 50MP camera, 6.6-inch Super AMOLED display. Priced at BRL 2,699 in Brazil. Learn more.

item.title: 5 Easy Ways to Check Your Airtel Number
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Want to know your Airtel number? Discover these quick and simple methods to find it.

How to Check Your Airtel Number Using 5 Different Methods

Airtel, a leading telecom operator, offers multiple ways for users to check their mobile numbers. Whether you are new to Airtel or just need a quick reminder, these five methods will help you easily find your number.

1. Airtel Number Check via USSD Code

USSD codes provide instant information directly from your mobile network. Here’s how to check your Airtel number using a USSD code:

  1. Open the Phone or Dialer app.
  2. Dial 1211# or *282#.
  3. A pop-up message will display your Airtel number.

2. Airtel Number Check via Airtel Thanks App

The Airtel Thanks app offers a comprehensive overview of your Airtel services, including your mobile number. Follow these steps:

  1. Download and install the Airtel Thanks app.
  2. Open the app and sign in to your Airtel account.
  3. Your Airtel number will be displayed at the top of the screen.

3. Airtel Number Check via Customer Care Support

You can also call Airtel customer care to know your number. Here’s how:

  1. Open the Phone or Dialer app.
  2. Dial 121 or 198.
  3. Press 1 for mobile services.
  4. The IVR will inform you about your Airtel number along with other account details.

4. Airtel Number Check by Calling Another Mobile

Another simple method is to call a friend or family member from your Airtel number. They can tell you the number that appears on their phone.

5. Airtel Number Check via Settings Menu on Mobile

If you use an iPhone, you can find your Airtel number in the Settings menu:

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Navigate to the Phone section.
  3. Your Airtel number will be listed under "My Number."

Conclusion

With these five methods, checking your Airtel number is quick and easy. Whether you prefer using a USSD code, an app, or a phone call, you can always find your number with minimal effort.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how to check your Airtel number using USSD codes, the Airtel Thanks app, customer care, calling another phone, and your phone settings.

item.title: How to Check Your BSNL Number Using Different Methods
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
BSNL Number Check via USSD Code

USSD codes provide an instant way to check your BSNL mobile number. Here’s how:

  1. Open the Phone or Dialer app on your device.
  2. Dial *1# from your BSNL number.
  3. A pop-up message will display your BSNL number.

Other USSD codes include:

  • *555#
  • 5552#
  • *888#
  • 8881#
  • 8882#

BSNL Number Check via BSNL App

The BSNL Selfcare app helps you track your mobile number and other details. Follow these steps:

  1. Download and install the BSNL Selfcare app on your device.
  2. Register or sign in with your BSNL account.
  3. Your BSNL number and other details will be displayed at the top of the screen.

BSNL Number Check via Customer Care Support

Contacting customer care is another effective method:

  1. Open the Phone or Dialer app.
  2. Dial 1800-180-1503 or 1503 from your BSNL number.
  3. Press 1 for mobile services.
  4. The IVR will provide your BSNL number along with other details.

BSNL Number Check by Calling Another Mobile

You can also discover your BSNL number by calling or texting a friend or family member. They will see your number on their phone.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Did you forget your BSNL number? #separator_sa #site_title
Forgot your BSNL number? Learn easy methods to check your BSNL number using USSD codes, the BSNL app, customer care, and more.

item.title: How to Delete or Deactivate Your Instagram Account on Mobile or PC
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
How to Delete an Instagram Account on Mobile (Android or iOS)

  1. Open Instagram on your device.
  2. Go to your profile by tapping the Hamburger menu.
  3. Navigate to Account Centre > Personal Details.
  4. Select Account Ownership and Control > Deactivation or Deletion.
  5. Choose the account to delete, then tap Delete Account and continue.
  6. Select a reason, enter your password, and follow the on-screen instructions.

How to Delete an Instagram Account on a PC or Laptop

Method 1:

  1. Sign into Instagram via browser.
  2. Visit Instagram Deletion Page.
  3. Select a reason and re-enter your password.
  4. Click Delete.

Method 2:

  1. Open Instagram in your browser.
  2. Click the Hamburger menu and go to Accounts Centre.
  3. Navigate to Personal Details > Account Ownership and Control > Deactivation or Deletion.
  4. Select the account to delete, then click Delete Account.
  5. Enter your password and follow the prompts.

How to Deactivate an Instagram Account

Deactivate on Mobile (Android or iOS):

  1. Open Instagram on your device.
  2. Tap the Hamburger menu and go to Account Centre.
  3. Click Personal Details > Ownership and Control > Deactivation or Deletion.
  4. Select the profile to deactivate and tap Deactivate Account.
  5. Enter your password and follow the on-screen instructions.

Deactivate on PC or Laptop:

  1. Open Instagram on your browser.
  2. Select Accounts Centre from the Hamburger menu.
  3. Go to Personal Details > Account Ownership and Control > Deactivation or Deletion.
  4. Choose the account to deactivate and click Deactivate Account.
  5. Enter your password and follow the prompts.

How to Reactivate Instagram Account

If you change your mind, you can reactivate your account within 30 days by logging back into Instagram with your username and password.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how to delete or deactivate your Instagram account on mobile or PC. Follow simple steps to manage your Instagram presence easily.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Wearables: Everything You need to know
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Samsung Galaxy, latest Unpacked event in Paris introduced groundbreaking wearables, including the Galaxy Buds3 Pro, Watch Ultra, and the innovative Galaxy Ring. We delve into each flagship wearable to determine its value and impact.

Galaxy Buds3 Pro: 'Edgy' but Useful

The Samsung Galaxy Buds3 Pro features a sharp-cornered design, reminiscent of the Tesla Cybertruck. These earbuds come with unique "blade lights" on their stems, adding a futuristic touch. Despite their edgy appearance, the Buds3 Pro offers useful features:

  • Touch Controls: Control calls, music, and volume by pinching or swiping the blade stems.
  • Customizable LEDs: LED lights can be switched on or off and customized for notifications.
  • Sound Quality: Good sound quality with adjustable equalizer settings, though noise cancellation could be improved.
  • Voice Controls: Supports English and Korean, with Bixby integration for more interactive commands.
  • Real-Time Interpreter: Translates spoken language in real-time, useful in noisy environments.

Galaxy Watch Ultra: A Big Difference

The Galaxy Watch Ultra is Samsung’s rugged smartwatch designed for extreme conditions. Key features include:

  • Durable Build: 47mm display, titanium casing, and military-grade certification for environmental protection.
  • Advanced Metrics: Tracks health metrics, withstands up to 100 meters underwater, and operates in extreme temperatures.
  • Elegant Design: Available in titanium white, silver, and grey with orange accents.
  • Enhanced Navigation: Raised touch bezel with haptic feedback for precise control.

Galaxy Ring: A New Era

Samsung's Galaxy Ring stands out as the first smart ring from a major brand. Its features include:

  • Health Monitoring: Tracks heart rate, sleep, snoring, steps, menstrual cycle, and stress.
  • Gesture Controls: Allows alarm dismissal and photo capture via double pinch gestures.
  • Design and Fit: Available in nine sizes with a sleek, titanium finish in black, silver, and gold.
  • Battery Life: Lasts up to seven days with a unique charging case.

While the Galaxy Ring is currently available in limited markets, Samsung is working to expand its availability.

Verdict

Samsung’s Galaxy Buds3 Pro, Watch Ultra, and Ring are designed to complement its Galaxy AI platform and enhance its ecosystem. The Buds3 Pro and Watch Ultra are competitively priced compared to Apple's similar products. The Ring, priced at $399, represents a bold step into the smart ring market, with potential to become mainstream. Whether these wearables replace existing devices depends on user preferences and the features they prioritize.

item.title: TRAI's New Guidelines for Mobile Number Porting from July 1
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Users cannot port their mobile number if they replaced their SIM card in the last seven days.

Highlights

  • TRAI introduces new rules for SIM card porting.
  • The regulation takes effect on July 1.
  • Users can initiate porting by sending an SMS to 1900.

New TRAI Mobile Number Porting Guidelines

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) announced new amendments to mobile number portability (MNP) regulations last week. Starting July 1, users cannot port their mobile number to a different telecom operator if they have swapped or replaced their SIM card in the last seven days. This update aims to combat SIM swap fraud in the country.

Key Changes in MNP Regulations

The new rules, part of the Telecommunication Mobile Number Portability (Ninth Amendment) Regulations, 2024, introduce a critical change: a seven-day restriction on porting if a SIM card has been replaced or swapped. This measure seeks to curb fraudulent porting activities.

Additional Rejection Criteria for UPC:

  • The request for a Unique Porting Code (UPC) will be rejected if made within seven days of a SIM swap or replacement.
  • This UPC is essential for transferring a mobile number from one telecom operator to another.

How to Initiate Porting

Users can switch their service provider without changing their mobile number using the MNP facility. To start the porting process:

  1. Send an SMS in the format PORT<ten-digit mobile number> to 1900.
  2. Receive a UPC via SMS.
  3. Use this UPC to place an MNP request.

Background and Implementation

TRAI first introduced MNP regulations in 2009. The latest amendment, effective from July 1, aims to enhance security and prevent fraudulent activities associated with SIM swaps.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: TRAI's new mobile number porting rules, effective July 1, prevent porting within seven days of a SIM swap. Learn how to initiate porting via SMS.

item.title: iOS 18 : Top 6 Features You Need To Know!
item.dc:creator: themobiletimes.com
item.content:encoded:
Apple's upcoming iOS 18 is set to bring significant updates and enhancements to iPhones, combining advanced AI features, design overhauls, and app upgrades.

Key Features of iOS 18

  1. Enhanced AI and Siri:
    iOS 18 will introduce advanced AI functionalities, particularly through Siri. Utilizing large language models (LLMs), Siri will offer more conversational interactions, proactive intelligence, and the ability to handle more complex tasks. These improvements aim to transform Siri into a more versatile virtual assistant.
  2. Home Screen Customization:
    Users will now have more control over their home screens, with the ability to freely place app icons and widgets, breaking away from the traditional fixed grid system. This feature also includes new theming options, allowing for custom icon colors and a more personalized home screen experience.
  3. Control Center Overhaul:
    The Control Center will see significant changes, including the ability to create distinct function groups and resize widgets. Users will also be able to customize which widgets appear on the lock screen, enhancing usability and personalization.
  4. Photos App Redesign:
    The Photos app will undergo its most significant redesign yet, featuring a unified view that combines a photo grid and dates grid, alongside new filtering options to easily locate specific photos. This overhaul aims to streamline photo management and improve the user experience.
  5. Messages Enhancements:
    The Messages app will support new text effects, message scheduling, and expanded satellite communication capabilities. Additionally, it will integrate the RCS standard for richer messaging experiences with non-Apple devices, ensuring broader compatibility and functionality.
  6. App and Feature Updates:
    Several built-in apps, including Safari, Mail, and Wallet, will receive updates. Safari will feature an intelligent search tool and a potential "Web Eraser" function, while Mail will categorize emails more effectively. The Wallet app will introduce a "tap to cash" feature for easier payments.

Compatibility and Release

iOS 18 will be compatible with iPhones from the iPhone XS onwards, including all models equipped with the A12 Bionic chipset or newer. However, some advanced AI features will be exclusive to the iPhone 15 Pro models, which boast the A17 Pro chipset.

Apple plans to roll out iOS 18 later this year, following its announcement at WWDC 2024. While the initial release will include many new features, some, particularly those related to AI, will be introduced in subsequent updates.

For more details, visit the official iOS 18 preview page.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the latest features in iOS 18, including advanced AI enhancements, redesigned home screens, and upgraded apps. Learn what's new for your iPhone.

item.title: Vivo X Fold 3 Pro: Everything you need to know!
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Vivo enters the foldable smartphone market with its Vivo X Fold 3 Pro, offering a feature-packed alternative to Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 5. After using the phone for several weeks, it’s clear that Vivo hasn’t reinvented the wheel but has taken the foldable experience to the next level.

Vivo X Fold 3 Pro : Pricing and Storage

The Vivo X Fold 3 Pro, available with 16GB of RAM and 512GB of storage, is priced at Rs. 1,59,999. While this might seem high for an Android smartphone, it stands competitively against Samsung's offerings. The Galaxy Z Fold 5 starts at Rs. 1,54,999 for the same storage and goes up to Rs. 1,84,999 for the 1TB variant. Samsung’s non-foldable Galaxy S24 Ultra is also pricey at Rs. 1,29,999. Thus, the Vivo X Fold 3 Pro offers substantial value for its price.

Design: Thin is In

Dimensions:

  • Closed: 159.96 x 72.55 x 11.2mm
  • Open: 159.96 x 142.4 x 5.2mm
  • Weight: 236 grams
  • Color: Celestial Black

The Vivo X Fold 3 Pro emphasizes a sleek design. Its thin profile makes it stand out, whether closed or open. The Celestial Black finish adds a touch of elegance, making it a visually appealing device.

Vivo X Fold 3 Pro: Features and Performance

Vivo has packed the X Fold 3 Pro with top-tier specifications. The large 16GB RAM ensures smooth multitasking, while the 512GB storage provides ample space for apps and media. The foldable display offers an immersive viewing experience, perfect for media consumption and productivity tasks.

Is It Worth It?

While Vivo hasn’t revolutionized the foldable market, the X Fold 3 Pro’s combination of design, performance, and pricing makes it a compelling option. It stands as a strong competitor to Samsung’s offerings, especially for those seeking a premium foldable smartphone.

Conclusion

The Vivo X Fold 3 Pro, with its competitive pricing and robust features, provides a solid alternative in the foldable smartphone market. Its sleek design and substantial storage make it a worthy consideration for premium smartphone buyers in India.

item.title: How to Port Mobile Number to BSNL?
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
To Port Mobile Number to BSNL, Many users in India are considering switching to BSNL after recent tariff revisions by major telecom operators like Jio, Vi, and Airtel. With these companies increasing their recharge plan prices by an average of 15%, BSNL's more affordable plans are becoming an attractive option.

Why Consider BSNL?

  • Affordable Plans: BSNL offers cheaper recharge plans compared to other operators.
  • Network Availability: While currently operating on 2G/3G, BSNL’s 4G services are available in selected circles and are expected to roll out nationwide next month.

Step-by-Step Guide to Port Mobile Number to BSNL

Step 1: Obtain a Unique Porting Code (UPC)
Send an SMS to 1900 in the format: PORT <10 digit mobile number>. For example, send PORT 8888888888.

  • Jammu & Kashmir Users: Call 1900 instead of sending an SMS.
  • Validity: The UPC is valid for 15 days (30 days in Jammu & Kashmir, Assam, and North East licensed areas).

Step 2: Visit BSNL CSC or Authorized Franchisee/Retailer
Go to the nearest BSNL Customer Service Centre or authorized retailer to request mobile number porting.

Step 3: Fill in the Customer Application Form (CAF)
Complete the CAF and submit it along with the required porting fees. Currently, BSNL is not charging any fees for porting.

Step 4: Receive Your New BSNL SIM Card
Once your porting request is approved, BSNL will notify you of the date and time for the porting process. Insert your new BSNL SIM card at the specified time.

Need Help?

If you encounter any issues, contact BSNL’s toll-free number 1800-180-1503 or call 1503 for assistance.

Conclusion

Switching to BSNL can be a cost-effective move amidst rising telecom tariffs. Follow this simple guide to port your mobile number to BSNL and enjoy their affordable plans and expanding 4G network.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how to port mobile number to BSNL easily. Follow our step-by-step guide to switch from your current telecom operator and enjoy affordable plans.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Ring: Everything you need to know!
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Samsung unveiled its first smart ring, the Samsung Galaxy Ring, at the Galaxy Unpacked event in Paris. This new addition to Samsung's wearable lineup joins the Galaxy Watch7 and Galaxy Watch Ultra.

Expanding Wearable Portfolio

Price and Availability

The Galaxy Ring is priced at $399. Pre-orders start on July 10, with general availability from July 24. The ring is available in Titanium Black, Titanium Silver, and Titanium Gold, with nine size options.

Health and Fitness Features

The Galaxy Ring integrates health and fitness features via the Samsung Health app, offering seamless access to health data without a subscription. Key features include:

  • Sleep Analysis: Sleep score, snoring data, movement during sleep, sleep latency, heart rate, and respiratory rate.
  • 24/7 Health Monitoring: Continuous health tracking with a weight of 2.3 to 3.0 grams.
  • Water Resistance: 10ATM water resistance and Titanium Grade 5 finish.
  • Battery Life: Up to seven days, with a charging case featuring LED status indication.
  • Temperature Sensor: Includes Cycle Tracking feature.
  • Energy Score: Evaluates physical and mental conditions based on sleep, activity, and heart rate variability.
  • Heart Health Monitoring: Real-time heart rate alerts and Live Heart Rate check.
  • Fitness Tracking: Auto Workout Detection for walking and running, and Inactive Alert for daily fitness reminders.

Additional Features

The Galaxy Ring includes a double pinch gesture for controlling functionalities on connected Galaxy smartphones, such as taking photos and dismissing alarms. It also features Find My Ring for locating the ring via the Samsung Find network.

Specifications

  • Colors: Titanium Black, Titanium Silver, Titanium Gold
  • Dimensions & Weight: 7.0 mm x 2.6 mm, 2.3g (Size 5) to 3.0g (Size 13)
  • Sizing: Sizes 5 to 13
  • Memory: 8MB
  • Battery: 18mAh (Size 5) - 23.5mAh (Size 13), Charging Case: 361 mAh
  • Sensors: Accelerometer, PPG, Skin Temperature
  • Connectivity: BLE 5.4
  • Durability: 10 ATM, IP68 / Titanium grade 5
  • Charging Case: 48.9mm x 48.9mm x 24.51mm, 61.3g

The Samsung Galaxy Ring marks a significant step in wearable technology, offering a blend of advanced health monitoring and sleek design.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover Samsung Galaxy Ring, a groundbreaking smart wearable priced at $399, featuring comprehensive health and fitness monitoring.

item.title: DoT Stops More Than 24000 Fraudulent Mobile Connections?
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Due to suspected fraudulent activities, DoT( Department of Telecommunications) suspended 24,229 Fraudulent Mobile connections and blocked 42 unique International Mobile Equipment Identity (IMEI) numbers. These actions were linked to just three mobile numbers.

Crackdown on Fraudulent Activities

The disconnection follows complaints made by the public on the government's Chaksu portal. Launched in March, Chaksu allows citizens to report suspicious mobile numbers used for fraudulent communication via calls, SMS, or social media platforms like WhatsApp. Once reported, these numbers undergo re-verification; if they fail, they are disconnected.

Details of the Suspended Connections

Many of the mobile numbers in the latest disconnection batch showed suspected fraudulent activity and were disconnected simultaneously. The IMEI numbers, unique 15-digit serial identifiers for devices, showed a disturbing trend of multiple numbers linked to single devices. One IMEI was linked to 4,146 mobile numbers, highlighting the scale of the issue.

Telecom Service Provider Directives

Telecom service providers have been instructed to block these IMEI numbers on a pan-India basis to prevent further fraudulent activities.

Part of a Broader Initiative

This action is part of the DoT's 100-day agenda to combat the rising tide of spam calls and text messages through multiple interventions.

Conclusion

The DoT's recent actions demonstrate a significant step towards curbing mobile fraud in India. By leveraging public reports and enforcing strict re-verification measures, the DoT aims to protect consumers from fraudulent activities and enhance the security of mobile communications.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The DoT suspended 24,229 Fraudulent Mobile Connections linked to three numbers. Learn about the crackdown on mobile fraud and the measures being implemented.

item.title: Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 Review: Everything you need to know!
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Review of Asus ROG Zephyrus G16: High Specs, Mixed Results

The Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 has emerged as a prominent choice in the thin gaming laptop market. Featuring a Core Ultra 9 185H and a GeForce RTX 4090, this high-end model boasts impressive specs. However, does its performance live up to the expectations?

Vapor Chamber Cooling: A Step Up?

Asus has equipped the most powerful ROG Zephyrus G16 models with vapor chamber cooling. This system replaces the conventional cooling unit found in RTX 4070 configurations, which uses three fans and heat pipes. The key question is whether this new cooling method significantly improves performance and emissions.

Our review unit, labeled GU605MY, costs $3,649. It comes with 32 GB of RAM and a 240 Hz OLED screen. This review focuses on the differences between the RTX 4070 and the high-end model's performance and emissions.

Performance Comparison

The high-end ROG Zephyrus G16 configuration aims to deliver top-tier gaming performance. The Core Ultra 9 185H, combined with the RTX 4090, promises exceptional speed and graphics. However, the actual performance gains from the vapor chamber cooling compared to the conventional cooling in the RTX 4070 model are marginal.

Emissions and Cooling Efficiency

While the vapor chamber cooling system should theoretically provide better thermal management, our tests show that the improvement is not as significant as expected. The system manages to keep the laptop cooler under heavy loads but still struggles with high temperatures during extended gaming sessions. The emissions, in terms of noise and heat, remain a concern for a laptop in this price range.

Additional Features

The ROG Zephyrus G16 continues to impress with its OLED display, delivering vibrant colors and a smooth 240 Hz refresh rate. The build quality remains solid, and the laptop maintains a sleek, portable design.

Asus plans to release an updated model with AMD’s new Strix Point processors (Zen5), though these will only feature the RTX 4070 GPU. This upcoming update might offer a more balanced performance without the high-end price tag.

Conclusion

The Asus ROG Zephyrus G16, with its slim design and high-end specifications, shows promise. However, the potential of its vapor chamber cooling system remains unfulfilled. While it delivers powerful performance, the thermal management and emissions could use further refinement.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: "Discover the Asus ROG Zephyrus G16 with RTX 4090 and Core Ultra 9 185H. Uncover its cooling performance, potential compromises, and overall gaming experience."

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra: Samsung Steps in the Game?
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra: Features and Specs

Samsung recently unveiled the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra, its newest smartwatch designed to challenge the Apple Watch Ultra 2. Packed with cutting-edge technology and robust features, this smartwatch aims to set new standards in the wearable market.

Premium Build and Display

The Galaxy Watch Ultra features titanium cushioned frames and a 1.5-inch Sapphire Crystal screen. The AMOLED panel offers a resolution of 480×480 pixels and an impressive brightness of 3,000 nits, ensuring clarity in all lighting conditions.

First to Run on One UI 6

This smartwatch is the first to run One UI 6 based on WearOS, right out of the box. Samsung’s latest software introduces several new health-tracking features, including:

  • Energy Score: Similar to Fitbit’s Readiness Score, it evaluates your physical and mental conditions across sleep, activity, sleeping heart rate, and variability.
  • Sleep Score and Metrics: Offers detailed analysis with new metrics like movement during sleep, sleep latency, and heart and respiratory rates. It can also detect Sleep Apnea in selected regions.
  • Advanced Glycation (AGEs) Index: Provides insights into your metabolic health and biological aging process.

Durability and Performance

The Galaxy Watch Ultra boasts 10ATM water resistance and IP68 dust and water resistance. It also includes an additional physical button for enhanced functionality.

Powered by the Exynos W1000 chipset, built on a 3nm process, the watch comes with 2GB RAM and 32GB storage. The 590mAh battery ensures up to 100 hours of usage with power-saving mode enabled.

Advanced Sensors and Availability

The new BioActive Sensor is 30% more accurate than previous models. The watch also includes a temperature sensor, accelerometer, barometer, gyro sensor, and light sensor. Available in one screen size, the Galaxy Watch Ultra is priced at $649 (approx. Rs 54,200) and available for purchase from July 12.

With these features, the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra is poised to be a formidable competitor in the smartwatch market, offering users a comprehensive and advanced wearable experience.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra rivals Apple Watch Ultra 2 with advanced features like One UI 6, 10ATM water resistance, and a 590mAh battery. Learn more!

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 and Z Flip 6: Everything you need to know
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
On July 10, Samsung introduced the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 and Z Flip 6 in India. These foldable devices, part of the 2024 Galaxy Z series, come with significant software and AI enhancements.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 and Z Flip 6: New Features and Improvements

The Galaxy Z Fold 6 and Z Flip 6 feature the Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 for Galaxy, improved design for better ergonomics and durability, and enhanced Galaxy AI capabilities tailored for foldable devices.

Galaxy Z Fold 6 Specifications:

  • Cover Display: 6.3-inch HD+ AMOLED, 120Hz adaptive refresh rate
  • Main Display: 7.6-inch QXGA+ AMOLED, 120Hz adaptive refresh rate
  • Processor: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 for Galaxy
  • RAM: 12GB
  • Storage Options: 256GB, 512GB, 1TB
  • Cameras: 50MP rear with OIS, 12MP ultra-wide, 10MP telephoto (3x optical zoom), 10MP front (cover), 4MP front (under-display)
  • Battery: 4,400mAh
  • Protection: IP48, Corning Gorilla Glass Victus 2

Galaxy Z Flip 6 Specifications:

  • Cover Display: 3.4-inch Super AMOLED, 60Hz refresh rate
  • Main Display: 6.7-inch FHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 120Hz adaptive refresh rate
  • Processor: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 for Galaxy
  • RAM: 12GB
  • Storage Options: 256GB, 512GB
  • Cameras: 50MP primary with OIS, 12MP ultra-wide, 10MP front
  • Battery: 4,000mAh
  • Protection: IP48, Corning Gorilla Glass Victus 2

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 and Z Flip 6: Pricing and Variants

Galaxy Z Fold 6:

  • 12GB RAM + 256GB Storage: Rs 164,999
  • 12GB RAM + 512GB Storage: Rs 176,999
  • 12GB RAM + 1TB Storage: Rs 200,999
  • Colors: Silver Shadow, Navy, Pink, Black (exclusive), White (Exclusive)

Galaxy Z Flip 6:

  • 12GB RAM + 256GB Storage: Rs 109,999
  • 12GB RAM + 512GB Storage: Rs 121,999
  • Colors: Blue, Mint, Silver Shadow, Black (exclusive), White (exclusive), Peach (exclusive)

Pre-Order Offers

Customers who pre-order the Galaxy Z Fold 6 or Z Flip 6 by July 24 can enjoy several benefits:

  • Cashback: Rs 8,000 with HDFC Bank cards
  • No-Cost EMI: Nine-month no-interest EMI
  • Upgrade Bonus: Rs 8,000 upgrade bonus and Rs 15,000 for existing Samsung flagship users
