Available for pre-order starting at 1189 EUR (around $1000 USD), the TUXEDO InfinityFlex 14 will begin shipping in mid-September 2024.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: TUXEDO launches its first 3-in-1 convertible Linux laptop, the InfinityFlex 14, featuring a 360° Full HD touchscreen, Intel Core i5, and up to 64GB RAM.

item.title: Apple Glass: Everything We Know About Apple's Revolutionary AR Glasses
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Apple Glass, the much-anticipated AR glasses from Apple, are set to redefine how we interact with the digital world. Building on the foundation laid by the Apple Vision Pro and the visionOS platform, Apple Glass aims to bring augmented reality to everyday life, allowing users to see digital information overlaid directly on their surroundings through transparent lenses.

Apple Glass Features

Though still in development, Apple Glass promises to offer groundbreaking features:

  • Augmented Reality Display: Information will be displayed on both lenses, integrating seamlessly with the real world.
  • iPhone-Dependent Processing: Like the original Apple Watch, Apple Glass will rely on iPhone for data processing, allowing the glasses to remain lightweight and stylish.
  • Gesture Controls: Expect intuitive gesture-based interactions, enhancing the AR experience without the need for physical controllers.
  • No Cameras, Just LiDAR: Apple is likely to skip traditional cameras, opting instead for LiDAR sensors to map environments and display AR content.
  • Prescription Lenses: Apple will likely offer prescription lenses, ensuring that the glasses are accessible to a broader audience.
  • Starboard UI: The interface, reportedly called "Starboard," will be optimized for AR, providing a unique user experience tailored to wearable tech.

A Long Road Ahead

The journey to Apple Glass is long and complex. Apple Vision Pro, launching in 2024, represents the first step towards this vision. The Vision Pro is a mixed reality headset that blends virtual and augmented reality, laying the groundwork for the more advanced and portable Apple Glass.

Design and Functionality

Designing a wearable that’s both functional and fashionable is no easy task. Apple is expected to prioritize a sleek, lightweight design, possibly offering both plastic and metal frame options. The company’s focus will be on creating a device that integrates effortlessly into daily life while offering powerful AR capabilities.

Battery Life and Processing
Balancing battery life with performance will be crucial. By relying on the iPhone for heavy processing, Apple Glass can remain light and energy-efficient, potentially offering several hours of use on a single charge.

Privacy Concerns
Apple Glass is likely to avoid the privacy pitfalls associated with AR devices by using LiDAR instead of cameras. This approach will prevent the glasses from capturing video, addressing concerns that plagued earlier products like Google Glass.

The Future of Wearables

Apple Glass represents a significant leap forward in wearable technology. While other companies have made strides in AR, Apple’s integration of AR into its ecosystem sets it apart. The company is taking a cautious approach, ensuring that when Apple Glass finally arrives, it will meet the high standards of quality and functionality that Apple users expect.

Release Date and Pricing
Rumors suggest that Apple Glass might not hit the market until 2030. The technology required to make such a product feasible is still in development, and Apple is likely to take its time perfecting the device. Pricing remains speculative, but given the Vision Pro's $3,499 starting price, Apple Glass could also come with a premium price tag.

Conclusion

Apple Glass is poised to be a game-changer in the realm of augmented reality, blending cutting-edge technology with everyday usability. While its release is still several years away, the groundwork being laid today with Apple Vision Pro and visionOS is setting the stage for a revolutionary product that could change how we see and interact with the world.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the latest updates on Apple Glass, the upcoming AR glasses that will redefine digital interaction. Expected release, features, and more.

item.title: Anker has unveiled its latest addition to the Prime lineup
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Anker has unveiled its latest addition to the Prime lineup, the Anker Prime 250W 6-Port GaN Charger, now available in the US, UK, and select EU markets.

Key Features and Specifications

The Anker Prime 250W GaN charger is designed for high-efficiency charging, featuring four USB-C ports and two USB-A ports. Here’s what it offers:

  • 140W Output on USB-C1: The first USB-C port can charge a MacBook Pro 16 up to 50% in just 25 minutes. It fully charges the device in 1.5 hours.
  • 100W Power on Other USB-C Ports: Each of the remaining USB-C ports delivers up to 100W, suitable for charging laptops, tablets, and smartphones.
  • 22.5W Output on USB-A Ports: The two USB-A ports support fast charging at 22.5W.

Advanced Features

This charger isn’t just powerful—it’s smart:

  • 2.26-Inch LCD Display: The charger comes with an LCD that shows charging status, speed, time, and date.
  • Control Dial with Four Modes: Users can switch between AI Power, Low Current, Port Priority, and Dual-Laptop modes, optimizing power distribution based on needs.
  • AI Power: Automatically adjusts output based on connected devices.
  • Low Current: Ideal for overnight charging.
  • Port Priority: Prioritizes 100W output on the first USB-C port.
  • Dual-Laptop: Distributes 70W across two USB-C outputs for charging two laptops simultaneously.

Pricing and Availability

The Anker Prime 250W GaN charger is priced at $169.99 in the US, £169.99 in the UK, and €159.99 in the EU. Anker offers an introductory discount of up to $30 (around 15% off), though the duration of this offer is not specified. The charger will be available starting August 25 in the US, UK, and EU.

This powerful and versatile charger is set to meet the needs of users looking for efficient and intelligent charging solutions for multiple devices.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Anker launches a 250W GaN charger with six ports, including four USB-C and two USB-A, available in the US, UK, and EU. Introductory discounts apply.

item.title: BoAt reaches 50 Million 'Made in India' Milestone: Here's how
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
BoAt, India's leading audio and wearable brand, has surpassed the 50 million mark for 'Made in India' products. This achievement comes amid a challenging environment marked by declining sales and limited product innovation in the wearable sector.

Despite a 9.8% year-over-year (Y-o-Y) decline in shipments, BoAt remains the market leader with a 26.7% market share, according to IDC data. The brand manufactures 70-75% of its audio and smart wearable products within India, through a joint venture with Dixon Technologies.

Local Manufacturing and Innovation

BoAt's collaboration with Dixon Technologies has enabled the company to optimize its supply chain, reduce lead times, and enhance product quality. This focus on local manufacturing aligns with the 'Make in India' initiative and reduces dependency on foreign production.

Sameer Mehta, Co-founder and CEO of BoAt, highlighted the importance of this milestone. "Crossing the 5-crore mark in Made in India products is a momentous occasion for BoAt. It reflects our unwavering commitment to the ‘Make in India’ initiative and our confidence in the capabilities of Indian manufacturing," Mehta said.

Market Challenges and Strategy Shifts

India’s wearable device market saw a 10% Y-o-Y decline in Q2 2024, with 29.5 million units shipped. The first half of 2024 recorded a 4.7% decline, shipping 55.1 million units. Analysts attribute this drop to limited innovation and vendors’ cautious approach to inventory management.

In response to these challenges, BoAt is investing in local research and development (R&D) through BoAt Labs. This innovation center has established partnerships with global technology leaders like Google, Qualcomm, and Dolby. By focusing on local design and engineering, BoAt aims to create products tailored to the Indian market's unique needs.

Looking Forward

As the market faces low differentiation and innovation, BoAt is shifting its strategy to focus on higher-end devices. The brand is avoiding entry-level products and instead aims to deliver mature hardware and sophisticated algorithms.

While the wearable market struggles, BoAt’s continued investment in local manufacturing and R&D positions it well for future growth. As the festive season approaches, the industry anticipates new model launches that may help stabilize the market.

BoAt’s success in crossing the 50 million 'Made in India' milestone underscores its resilience and commitment to quality, even in a challenging market.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: BoAt crosses 50 million 'Made in India' products despite a 9.8% Y-o-Y decline in shipments, maintaining a 26.7% market share. IPO-bound parent plans global expansion.

item.title: Discover the Dell Latitude 9450, a stunning 2-in-1
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Dell Latitude 9450 2-in-1 is the latest addition to Dell's premium business laptop lineup, designed for professionals seeking a powerful and compact device. The laptop is tailored for C-suite executives, offering a sleek design and cutting-edge hardware.

Price and Availability

The Dell Latitude 9450 starts at ₹2,60,150 and goes up to ₹3,06,800 for the top variant. You can customize it with additional features like 5G connectivity. The laptop is available on the Dell Online Store and through authorized retailers across India.

Design and Build Quality

The Dell Latitude 9450 sports a minimalist design, featuring a CNC-milled aluminum body with diamond-cut edges. Its Graphite-colored matte finish prevents fingerprint smudges, and the 2-in-1 form factor allows seamless conversion between laptop and tablet modes. Despite its compact design, the laptop weighs 1.5 kg, prioritizing durability.

The 14-inch Quad HD+ IPS display offers excellent brightness at 500 nits, making it usable even under direct sunlight. The display is touch-enabled with an anti-smudge coating, ensuring a smooth user experience. However, the 60Hz refresh rate may feel limiting for some users.

Performance and Hardware

The laptop is powered by Intel's latest Core Ultra 7 165U processor from the Meteor Lake series, offering a mix of performance and efficiency cores. It handles multitasking effortlessly, making it ideal for professionals who need a reliable machine for demanding workloads. The 16GB LPDDR5X RAM and 1TB PCIe Gen 4 NVMe storage ensure fast performance and ample storage space.

Privacy and Security Features

The Dell Latitude 9450 excels in privacy features, crucial for business users. It includes a built-in camera privacy shutter and Dell Optimizer’s Presence Detection, which offers Onlooker Detection and automatic device locking/unlocking based on your proximity.

Verdict

The Dell Latitude 9450 2-in-1 is an excellent choice for professionals who need a powerful, compact, and feature-rich laptop. Its premium design, robust hardware, and strong privacy features make it a top contender in the business laptop market, despite its higher price tag.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the Dell Latitude 9450 2-in-1, a premium business laptop with a sleek design, powerful Intel Core Ultra 7 chipset, and robust privacy features.

item.title: Vi is taking good steps to disrupt the Indian market
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Vi (Vodafone-Idea) is taking bold steps to disrupt the Indian telecom market, signaling a direct challenge to industry giants Jio and Airtel. With BSNL's 5G launch already heating up the competition, Vi's strategy to offer more attractive 5G pricing could significantly shift market dynamics.

During a recent earnings call, Vi CEO Akshay Mundra hinted at competitive pricing for their upcoming 5G services. While still in the early stages of deployment, Mundra's remarks suggest a deliberate move to attract users away from Jio and Airtel. He stated, "We are considering pricing that could be more attractive than what's currently available."

Although Vi's exact 5G pricing remains unclear, the company is focused on building its infrastructure and assessing market needs. This strategy contrasts with Jio and Airtel's higher initial 5G prices, which aim to boost their average revenue per user (ARPU).

Vi faces additional challenges, including the replacement of Chinese-made equipment due to regulatory restrictions, complicating their 5G rollout. Financially, the company is also under pressure, having reported a significant loss of ₹6,434.4 crore last quarter. However, recent tariff hikes and a steady ARPU offer some optimism.

As BSNL and Vi intensify their efforts, India's telecom landscape is set for a major transformation, promising more choices and potentially lower costs for mobile users.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Vi competitive 5G pricing strategy and BSNL's 5G launch are set to shake up India's telecom industry, challenging Jio and Airtel's dominance

item.title: Epic Games Store is now available for download on Android devices globally
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Epic Games has officially launched its digital storefront on mobile devices, marking a significant expansion for the company. The Epic Games Store is now available for download on Android devices globally, while iPhone users in the European Union can also access the store.

In a press release, Epic revealed that the process of installing the store on mobile devices is intentionally complex, with the company blaming Apple and Google for introducing poor-quality installation experiences. Despite these hurdles, the launch marks a critical step in Epic's efforts to expand its platform.

The mobile version of the Epic Games Store mirrors the desktop experience, offering developers an 88/12 revenue share. Additionally, Epic announced the return of Fortnite to mobile devices, available through its store and the AltStore for EU users. Alongside Fortnite, games like Fall Guys and Rocket League Sideswipe are also now accessible on Android and EU iPhones.

Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney expressed gratitude to the European Commission for facilitating the launch, emphasizing the growing competition in the mobile ecosystem. This move represents a significant victory for developers and consumers alike, offering more choices and opportunities in the mobile gaming market.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Epic Games Store is now live on Android devices worldwide and iPhones in the EU, bringing Fortnite, Fall Guys, and more to mobile users.

item.title: Age of Empires Mobile Launches on Oct 17: Pre-Register Now
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
In collaboration with Xbox Game Studios, Timi Games is bringing the classic Age of Empires experience to mobile devices. Age of Empires Mobile is set to launch on October 17, 2024, on both Android and iOS platforms.

Pre-Registration Now Open

Fans can now pre-register for the game on the Google Play Store or pre-order on the Apple App Store. Early birds will receive exclusive in-game rewards, such as extra building materials, speed-up tokens, and Cleopatra VII as a special hero character.

What to Expect?

This mobile version blends the beloved elements of the original Age of Empires with the convenience of mobile gaming. Players can build empires in a medieval setting, choosing from various civilizations such as the French, Byzantines, Romans, and Chinese. The game also features multiple single-player modes and historical figures like Barbarossa and Alexander the Great, adding depth to the strategic gameplay.

Age of Empires Mobile is poised to bring the rich history and strategy of the PC classic to a new generation of gamers on the go. Don’t miss the opportunity to start building your empire—pre-register today!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Age of Empires Mobile hits iOS and Android on October 17. Pre-register now on Google Play and Apple App Store for exclusive in-game rewards.

item.title: Lava, a home-grown smartphone maker, sees opportunity in the downturn
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
India's wearables market faced its first-ever decline in Q2 2024, with a 10% year-on-year drop to 29.5 million units. Despite this, Lava, a home-grown smartphone maker, sees an opportunity in the downturn. With its recent entry into the wearables sector, the company aims to secure a 20% market share over the next few years.

Strategic Move into Wearables

Lava’s move into wearables aligns with its core strengths in the smartphone industry. “We decided to enter wearables due to synergies with our smartphone business,” said Sunil Raina, Executive Director at Lava. The company leverages its supply chain, R&D, and customer service to create a competitive edge.

Focus on Affordable Segment

Lava is concentrating on the sub-Rs 5,000 wearables segment, where it currently holds a low single-digit market share. The company plans to increase this share to 10-15% in the next few years by addressing the market's innovation gap.

Overcoming Market Challenges

The wearables market in India is saturated with limited innovation, leading to cautious vendor strategies and a focus on clearing old inventory. Lava, however, is investing in R&D to improve product quality and meet customer expectations. This approach, the company believes, will drive growth even in a declining market.

Future Outlook

Lava’s strategy includes launching new smartwatches and audio products ahead of the festival season. The company also plans to integrate AI into its upcoming wearables, enhancing the user experience. As the market matures, Lava expects consumers to pay more for quality, potentially leading to a market rebound.

With its roots firmly in India, Lava's local manufacturing and in-house R&D give it a unique advantage. The company’s customer-first approach and focus on innovation could set it apart in a competitive landscape.

item.title: The Future of Wearable Tech: 3D Circuits
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Medical technology is fast-moving; innovation is at its very core. Already, wearable devices have revolutionized health due to the possibility of continuous monitoring and sharing of data in real time. However, the future holds much more in store: smaller, smarter, and more comfortable devices.

Introducing 3D Circuits: A Revolutionary Technology

Meet 3DCircuits or, more specifically, 3DMID—Mechatronic Integrated Device—a revolutionizing technology in which circuits are integrated into 3D-molded plastic that makes separate components and bulky connections of yesteryear. Just imagine the casing of your hearing aid being a circuit board itself. This innovation can ultimately redefine the wearable medical device.

Advantages of 3D-Circuits

Miniaturization
3D-Circuits make all wearable devices very compact. They realize, for example, basically invisible and comfortable-to-wear in-ear hearing aids.

More Function in the Smallest Space
Despite their compact size, 3D circuits can pack more features into a single device to offer increased performance that improves sound quality and lowers power consumption.

Seamless Design
The 3D circuits offer complete design freedom to customize devices for a perfect fit, thus enhancing both comfort and usability.

Trustworthy Solutions from HARTING
For over a decade now, HARTING has led in 3D-MID solutions for medical devices, starting from concept design through to manufacturing, offering integral services so that high-quality, reliable, and long-lasting 3D circuits are realized.

Be part of the Future of Wearable Medical Technology.
With the ever-changing landscape in medical technology, 3D circuits are sure to be at par. HARTING innovative solutions opens up an entirely different dimension of perspective on how the future of wearable medical devices is going to turn into tiny, smart, and comfortable devices for patients. The future of healthcare has arrived, and it's powered by 3D-Circuits.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover how 3D circuits are revolutionizing wearable medical devices, making them smaller, smarter, and more comfortable for patients.

item.title: Motorola Launches Moto G45 5G in India: What's New?
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Motorola has introduced a budget offering—the Moto G45 5G —in India. For ₹9,999, after all offers, be available from August 28 on Motorola's official website, Flipkart, and select retail stores, the brand is touting powerful features in this device.

Moto G45 5G: Key Features and Specifications

Performance
The Moto G45 5G comes with a Qualcomm Snapdragon 6s Gen 3 chipset for seamless performance. It hosts 13 5G bands, thus making the device future-ready.

Display
It comes with a 6.5-inch display running on a refresh rate of 120Hz and is further protected with Corning Gorilla Glass 3. This could give a very smooth viewing experience with added durability.

Camera
At the back, it comes fitted with a dual-camera setup: 50MP primary sensor and a 2MP depth sensor. Up front, there's a 16MP front camera for selfies.
Battery

A robust 5,000mAh battery guarantees long-lasting usage.

Audio and Design
The Moto G45 5G does come with Dolby Atmos surround sound and Hi-Res audio. On top of it, the phone has a vegan leather coating at the back that comes in colour variants brilliant blue, brilliant green and viva magenta.

Variants and Pricing
4GB RAM + 128GB Storage: ₹10,999
8GB RAM + 128GB Storage: ₹12,999

Introductory Offers
Instant Discount ₹1,000 on Axis Bank and IDFC First Bank credit cards and credit card EMI transactions is given by Motorola. This offer will be available from August 28 to September 10.

Availability
The Moto G45 5G can be availed from August 28 through the company's official website, Flipkart, and a few retail stores across India.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Motorola's new Moto G45 5G launched in India at ₹9,999. Check out its features, specs, and introductory offers. Available from August 28.

item.title: Apple to Launch its new lineup on Sept. 10
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Apple Inc. is all set to host its major event on September 10, when it will launch the much-awaited iPhone 16, Apple Watch Series 10, and refreshed AirPods. It would be Apple's tradition of launching new products every September when the iPhone 16 is pegged to go on sale by September 20.

Key Updates and Features
iPhone 16: With the new lineup of iPhone 16 models, Apple is transitioning to larger screens on the Pro models and adding some pretty cool camera features, one of which is a dedicated picture-taking button. There isn't much design change from the previous ones, though. To beef up the intelligence, Apple is adding a suite of AI tools under the banner "Apple Intelligence."

Apple Watch Series 10: The next version to arrive, in 2023, will give a thinner design and larger screens across the lineup—what many feel will be Apple's first big refresh for the wearable line.

AirPods: The company is releasing new versions of low-end and mid-tier AirPods. What's perhaps most interesting is the addition of noise cancellation for the first time to mid-tier AirPods, while the entry-level model gets its first update since 2019.

Financial Impact
It's a crucial launch for Apple after the company failed to see robust sales in the past few quarters. In addition, the September 20 release date of iPhone 16 seems tactful as part of the revenue will accrue during Apple's fiscal fourth quarter. Estimates by analysts had a 5% growth in sales for the said quarter and a more substantial revenue run during the holiday quarter.

What's Next for Macs?
Alongside the iPhone, Watch, and AirPods, Apple is getting ready to refresh its Mac lineup using the new M4 processors. Four new Mac models will be making their way through the testing phase for their launch later in the year. These will include new MacBook Pros, a smaller Mac mini, and refreshed iMacs—all of which will be equipped with an M4 chip.

Conclusion
If an event is as large as Apple's September 10th event, it will definitely be a real strong moment for the company with new innovations across many product lines. From the iPhone 16 to the Watch Series 10 and the remade AirPods, this technology firm is looking at consumer attention and nudging sales into the holiday season. Keep your eye out for these exciting new releases as Apple continues to shape the future of technology.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Apple Inc. is all set to host its major event on September 10, when it will launch the much-awaited iPhone 16, Apple Watch Series 10, and refreshed AirPods.

item.title: This is how Nanogenerator is revolutionizing Wearables.
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
A breakthrough nanogenerator from the University of Surrey's Advanced Technology Institute could soon power your wearable devices using your morning run. The innovation provides a massive 140-fold increase in power density compared with conventional nanogenerators and sets up highly efficient energy harvesting from everyday movements.

Highly Efficient Energy Harvesting
The new nanogenerator converts small mechanical energy, for instance, motion while walking or running, into much bulk electrical power. For example, if a regular nanogenerator produces 10 milliwatts of power, this advanced device could push the output to more than 1,000 milliwatts—dramatically changing energy harvesting in everyday applications.

Technology Advances
This triboelectric nanogenerator is based on the charge regeneration effect. It employs materials that become electrically charged upon contact and separation—similar to the static electricity formed when one rubs a balloon on hair. Efficiency has now been improved through tuning with 34 small energy collectors utilizing a readily mass-producible laser technique.

Future Implications
The potentials that lie in this technology are enormous. Indeed, self-powered sensors from smart home systems, which will never require battery replacement, might one day be equivalent in power to solar panels. The University of Surrey is also set to launch a company that will focus on the development of self-powered, non-invasive healthcare sensors using this triboelectric technology.

Sustainable Energy Solutions
The pace at which the number of Internet of Things (IoT) devices across the world is predicted to top 50 billion in the coming years means critical local green energy solutions such as this nanogenerator. This technology can possibly change the perception of small electronic device power and source it from sustainable and available sources in everyday mechanical movement.

The developed motion-powered nanogenerator is the first step towards integrative and sustainable solutions of global energy challenges to independent wireless operation of smart systems for healthcare or home security.

item.title: Slick in appearance is the XPS 13 by Dell
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Dell XPS 13 Copilot+ PC looks like a nice-looking Windows laptop powered by the Snapdragon X Elite chip, matching modern design with compelling performance to make it one of the finest picks for any fan of premier technology

Future-Ready Design
Slick in appearance is the XPS 13 because it comes with a full-metal unibody with ultra-thin bezels. The compact dimension and the stylish appearance befit the modern and the minimalist I/O might take some getting used to—particularly in the absence of a 3.5mm headphone jack.

Stunning Tandem OLED Display
The 13.4-inch 3K Tandem OLED touch display has decent color accuracy and brightness, so it is quite good for media consumption and creation. With a refresh rate of 60Hz, it may be one of the factors that disappoints its users who are looking for smoother graphics.

Performance and AI Capabilities
This newest XPS 13 comes equipped with the latest Qualcomm Snapdragon X Elite processor, up to 32GB RAM, and a 1TB NVMe SSD to provide users with strong performance for working on the go and in some light gaming. The operating NPU will handle all advanced AI features, but they need an internet connection to be performed.

All-Day Battery Life
Whereas Dell has claimed 16 hours, this gadget is rated to run from 8 to 10 hours on a single charge, thus making it reliable for a full workday without recharging.

Final Verdict
That comes in the form of the Dell XPS 13 Copilot+, a premium Windows laptop that finds space somewhere between the design of its future, powerful shoebox-class performance, and artificial innovation. While undoubtedly not without trade-offs, it is at least a great choice for those eyeing strong machines in small sizes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the Dell XPS 13 Copilot+, a compact powerhouse with Snapdragon X Elite, Tandem OLED display, and AI features. A premium Windows laptop for tech enthusiasts.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S24 FE: Everything you need to know
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Samsung is about to launch the next device in its all-time flagship series—that is, Galaxy S24 Fe. The device, based on quite some recent leaks, shall have been reported to have received the BIS certificate, showing that the launching in India might just be around the corner for this one. More than this, the thing that's on everybody's mind is the launch date of this much-hyped device.\

Performance: Exynos 2400e Chip
Galaxy S24 FE will be powered with an underclocked variation of Exynos 2400, the likes of Exynos 2400e, with more balance in performance concerning energy efficiency and AI capability.

Design and Display
The Galaxy S24 FE will largely look similar to its predecessor, the Galaxy S23 FE. Boasting a slightly curved metal frame, the smartphone will have a flat display with uniform bezels and a glass back. The scores include new color options to choose from, such as Yellow. For protection, the screen will be covered with Corning Gorilla Glass Victus Plus. It likely will pack a display with a 6.7-inch AMOLED panel and a peak brightness of 1,900 nits.

Camera and Imaging
The Galaxy S24 FE is said to get a 50MP primary sensor, a 12MP ultra-wide lens, and an 8MP telephoto shooter that has 3x zoom capability. It should get a 10MP selfie camera for selfies and video calls.

AI Features
Advanced features of Samsung AI that come with the Galaxy S24 FE include Live Translate, Generative Edit, Interpreter, and Summarizer, among others. All such features are smart, powered features that enhance the user experience.


The Galaxy S24 FE should be well-rounded and AI-enabled, packed with the latest Samsung hardware and advanced AI capabilities. Keep your eyes glued for this launch, as it most certainly will bring an interesting option when taking into account that flagship experiences will be much more affordable.

item.title: Acer India has rolled out the Nitro V 16
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Acer India has rolled out the country's first completely AI-enabled gaming laptop—the Nitro V 16. This trendsetter in technology is going to raise both gameplay and productivity to totally new heights by fusing top hardware with never-before AI features.
Power-Packed Performance

The Nitro V 16 is powered by AMD Ryzen R7 8845HS Series Processors. Housing robust gaming and application performance under the hood, it packs a combination of an NVIDIA GeForce 4060 GPU, 16GB DDR5 RAM, and a 1TB SSD at its beck and call, thereby making quite a force among gamers and power users.
AI-Powered Features

By Nitro V running on Windows 11, most AI-centric applications would be supported, along with the integration of Microsoft's Copilot chatbot. This will enable Acer's packed AI features, such as Purified Voice 2.0 noise reduction for clear audio over gaming sessions and calls, to make the device really show its value.
Vibrant Visuals, Immersive Audio

This is packed with a 16-inch WQXGA IPS LCD screen, featuring a refresh rate of 165Hz for beautiful visuals and packing 100 percent sRGB color accuracy. This is paired with DTS Ultra Audio and customizable sound modes to provide immersive audio with high impedance headphones.
Advanced Cooling System

Equipped with a professional cooling system, Acer has fitted dual fans and quad intakes and exhausts in the Nitro V 16. This particular design prevents performance throttling during heavy gaming sessions and keeps the laptop running easily.

Pricing and Availability

Acer Nitro V 16 is priced at Rs 1,09,999 in Obsidian Black. It's available on various e-commerce platforms and at Acer-exclusive stores across India.
Key Features at a Glance

AI-Enhanced Performance: It supports 233 TOPS for AI tasks.
Display: 16" WQXGA with a refresh rate of 165Hz.
Connectivity: Laced with USB 4, HDMI, Bluetooth 5.1, and Wi-Fi 6.
Battery: 59Wh battery, lasting up to 7 hours with 135W charging support.
Acer's Nitro V 16 pits its powerful hardware against innovative AI capabilities, increasing the bar by a notch for gaming laptops in India.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Acer has launched its first AI-based gaming laptop, Nitro V 16, in India, AI-Powered Gaming Laptop.

item.title: Engineering Innovations Driving the Future of Wearable Tech
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Wearable technology has greatly impacted the day-to-day life of an individual by putting convenience in tandem with advanced innovation. From fitness trackers to smartwatches, all these devices have been catapulted into the middle of lifestyles in the modern era. Herein lies a great deal of engineering behind the sleekness of user experience, hardware-software integration, and security measures.

Understanding Wearable Technology
Wearable technology is a term used for devices from smartwatches, augmented reality glasses, and others that are supposed to be carried on one's body. They are lightweight, functional, and depict real-time data. The major driving force has been growing demands for health monitoring, miniaturization of electronics, and sensors.

Design Considerations for Wearable Technologies
User-centred design
Ease of use and convenience are two qualities. An engineer's work directly impacts functionality, weight, and esthetics to make devices ergonomic and appealing.

Hardware interfacing
Correct choice of sensors, processors, and batteries. Power-efficient and reliable components allow the device to function correctly for an extended period.

Software Development
Here follow some of the tasks that wearables software carries out. It writes the code so that it integrates into the meager hardware, and it designs the user interface.

Implementation Challenges and Solutions
Manage Power
Since wearables are using very small batteries, power management is obviously the main hurdle. Therefore, engineers need to use low-power components in developing and optimizing software to enhance battery life.

Data Protection and Privacy
Security is paramount since wearables operate with sensitive information. Engineers use techniques like encryption and authentication to protect user data.

Thaw and Reconnect Wearables should communicate with other devices efficiently using such protocols as Bluetooth and Wi-Fi, but there the similarity ends.

Miniaturization and Ergonomics
Devices must be compact yet functional. Engineers face the challenge of integrating all components into a small, lightweight design.

The Future of Wearable Technology Engineering
AI and Machine Learning
AI will drive the future of wearables with personalized insights and smarter functionality. The engineers have to balance great processing with limited hardware. Advanced sensors and health monitoring devices Wearables will monitor more advanced health metrics, with the sensing playing the role that new uses will have for greater pre-emptive health. Flexible Electronics, and Smart Textiles The next frontier involves wearables embedded directly into clothing. Engineers would be tasked with creating stretchable and robust electronics that maintain comfort and usability. Wearable technology engineering is an up-and-coming field that sits at the juxtaposition between technology and user-centered design. The engineers in wearable technology create, design, and implement innovative, functional, and human-friendly devices concerning power management and data security. Through advancing technological possibilities, wearables will probably ever increase integration into our technology and lives, offering ever-newer levels of personalization and functionality.

item.title: YouTube Premium Prices Hiked: New Rates for Indian Users
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
YouTube Premium will bump up its prices across all subscription plans for individual, family, and student memberships in India. Already live on the website, the price hike is across prepaid subscriptions and recurring prepaid subscriptions.

YouTube Premium Pricing in India Updated

  • Student Plan: Increased from Rs79 to Rs89 per month.
  • One Month Subscription Plan: Increased from Rs 129 to Rs 149.
  • Family Plan: From Rs 189 per month to a huge Rs 299 per month.
  • Per Minute Prepaid Monthly: From Rs 139 to Rs 159.
  • Quarterly Plan-prepaid: This was hiked from Rs. 399 to Rs. 459.
  • Annual Prepaid Plan: from Rs 1290 to Rs 1490.

All in all, the increased price simply means that subscribers whose plans are due for renewal have to start paying at the new pricing. New users get a one-month free service before they are charged under the new pricing.

Why Subscribe to YouTube Premium?
YouTube Premium comes with several advantages:

  • Ad-Free Streaming: Stream ad-free videos and music.
  • Background Playback: Continue playing the content even when changing apps.
  • Picture-in-Picture Mode: This allows watching videos in a smaller window while using other apps.
  • Enhanced Video Quality: Watch improved videos in higher resolution.

Take note that these will be updated, and note how the new pricing affects your subscription.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: YouTube Premium prices have increased in India. Check out the updated rates for individual, family, and student plans and see how they compare.

item.title: Sutter Health Expands Sepsis Care with Wearable Technology
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Sutter Health is building upon the existing skill level for sepsis management within its ICUs by implementing FloPatch developed by Flosonics Medical portable Doppler ultrasound. The first-of-a-kind technology allows real-time data on blood flow, which is key to speed and accuracy in decision-making when it comes to sepsis—a condition that may result in rapid deterioration and even death by patients.

The Urgency of Real-Time Data for Sepsis Management
Every year, sepsis strikes 1.7 million Americans, and approximately 350,000 of them die. The best treatment outcomes occur when early and adequate interventions are administered; therefore, the clinician must obtain information instantaneously. Conventional methods for the prediction of fluid volumes result in many cases in the clinician making inappropriate treatment decisions. The FloPatch directly measures carotid artery blood flow and provides actionable information on a patient's fluid responsiveness.

Introducing the FloPatch for Critical Care Launching on September 9, 2024, across Sutter's Eden Medical Center and California Pacific Medical Center, FloPatch will be used in ICUs, EDs, and trauma centers. Sutter Health has identified champions who are critical care nurses to support a smooth and effective rollout to integrate this technology into clinical flows.

Potential to Influence Patient Outcomes Sutter Health wants to reduce the morbidity and mortality associated with sepsis by maintaining appropriate fluid management and preventing respiratory or kidney failure. In other words, the FloPatch may greatly help in saving patient outcomes, which may go up to thousands of lives each year, by availing real-time data. This is a huge leap into advanced technology adoption by Sutter Health, setting a new benchmark in the field of critical care regarding sepsis treatment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Sutter Health implements FloPatch wearable to enhance sepsis management, offering real-time data to improve critical care outcomes in ICUs.

item.title: Wearable AI Notetakers: Plaud's NotePin Transcribes Your Life and Beyond
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Introduction to Plaud NotePin: The Future of AI Notetaking

Plaud, an innovative AI company, has unveiled its latest product, the Plaud NotePin—a wearable AI-powered notetaker that could transform the way we record and recall our daily interactions. This small, versatile device, about the size of a lipstick tube, is designed to be worn as a necklace, wristwatch, or pin, making it both functional and discreet.

Key Features and Capabilities

The NotePin builds on the foundation set by its predecessor, the Plaud Note, a slim audio recorder that could be attached to a phone or carried in a pocket. The NotePin carries forward these capabilities but in a more wearable form factor. Here’s what you can expect from the device:

  • Real-Time Transcription: The NotePin records and transcribes conversations in real-time, making it ideal for busy professionals who need accurate records of meetings, networking events, or interviews.
  • Storage and Plans: The device allows up to 300 minutes of monthly recording with the basic plan, priced at $169. An annual $79 Pro plan offers up to 1,200 minutes and additional features, like speaker identification, for those needing more.
  • Easy Integration: The device can be worn in multiple ways, ensuring it fits seamlessly into your daily routine, whether clipped to your lapel or worn as an accessory.

The Vision: From Notetaker to Digital Twin

Plaud’s ambitions don’t stop at simple notetaking. The company envisions a future where the NotePin evolves into a tool for creating a digital twin—a virtual replica of yourself that could one day learn and mimic your personality, preferences, and interactions based on the data it gathers from your conversations.

According to Plaud CEO Nathan Hsu, the idea is to help users capture and connect with countless memories over time, eventually allowing AI to replicate human experiences and behaviors. While this vision is still a long way off, it represents a fascinating glimpse into the potential future of AI wearables.

The AI Wearable Landscape: Competition and Challenges

The NotePin enters a crowded and competitive market, where AI-powered wearables have had mixed success. Many early devices, like the Humane AI Pin and Rabbit R1, failed to gain traction, often criticized for poor functionality or impractical design. Even more successful products like Meta’s Ray-Ban smart glasses have struggled with AI integration.

Moreover, consumers are becoming increasingly wary of devices that listen and record constantly, raising concerns about privacy and security. The NotePin, while offering encrypted cloud services, isn’t entirely secure—losing the device could potentially expose sensitive information.

Practical Considerations and Consumer Reception

Despite the futuristic promise of AI wearables like the NotePin, there are significant hurdles to widespread adoption. For one, AI transcription services are not infallible. Issues with accuracy, particularly when it comes to accents and complex speech patterns, can result in errors and misunderstandings. Users relying on these devices for critical tasks might find themselves facing embarrassing mistakes or even professional setbacks.

Additionally, there are privacy concerns. Even though Plaud assures users of encryption and a "find my device" feature, the prospect of losing such a device and the data it holds is a risk. Moreover, the question of whether users are ready to embrace a device that is always listening remains unanswered.

Conclusion: A Glimpse Into the Future

Plaud’s NotePin offers a compelling use case for AI in everyday life, particularly for those seeking to enhance productivity and keep meticulous records of their interactions. However, the success of this device will depend not only on its technical capabilities but also on consumer trust and willingness to adopt such technology.

As AI continues to evolve, the concept of a digital twin could become a reality, reshaping how we think about memory, identity, and the role of technology in our lives. For now, though, Plaud's NotePin is a bold step towards that future, even if it still faces the significant challenges that come with breaking new ground in AI wearables.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Plaud’s AI-powered NotePin, is a wearable device that records, transcribes, and summarizes conversations. Learn about its future role in creating digital twins.

item.title: Google Pixel Watch 3: A New Era in Wearable Health Tech
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Google is stepping up its game in the wearable tech market with the introduction of the Pixel Watch 3. This latest smartwatch is not just about telling time or tracking your steps—it’s pioneering a potentially life-saving feature: Loss of Pulse Detection.

Revolutionary Health Monitoring

The Pixel Watch 3 is Google’s third-generation smartwatch, and it aims to redefine health monitoring by detecting a loss of pulse, which could be a critical warning sign of cardiac arrest. This feature works by analyzing data from the watch’s photoplethysmography (PPG) sensor, which monitors blood flow under the skin. If the watch detects an anomaly, it sends an alert to check on the user and, if necessary, automatically contacts emergency services. While this feature is initially available only in Europe, Google is working to bring it to the U.S.

This advancement highlights Google's focus on integrating advanced health features into its wearables. According to Sandeep Waraich, product management lead for Google Wearables, the ambition behind this feature is immense, intending to potentially save lives by providing immediate assistance during cardiac emergencies.

Overcoming Challenges in Wearable Tech

The Pixel Watch 3’s development wasn’t without challenges. Google has faced stiff competition in the wearable market, particularly from industry giants like Apple, Garmin, and Samsung. Despite these obstacles, Wear OS, the operating system powering the Pixel Watch, has seen significant growth—40% in 2023 alone, according to Waraich.

Battery life remains a critical challenge. The continuous heart rate monitoring required for features like Loss of Pulse Detection demands significant power. To address this, Google has implemented a hybrid architecture in the Pixel Watch 3, where a low-power processor handles routine tasks, allowing the main processor to conserve energy. This approach, combined with the latest battery optimization technologies from its acquisition of KoruLab, helps extend the watch’s battery life. The Pixel Watch 3 comes in two sizes, with the larger 45-mm model boasting a battery that’s 35% bigger than the 41-mm version.

Despite these enhancements, Google remains cautious, maintaining the same 24-hour battery life claim as the original Pixel Watch. However, the Battery Saver mode now extends this to 36 hours without compromising essential health tracking features.

Bridging Innovation and Practicality

The Pixel Watch 3 is designed not only for health monitoring but also for a range of other smart functionalities. The watch supports multi-device interactions, such as controlling Google Home devices and viewing Nest camera feeds directly from the wrist. This versatility makes the Pixel Watch 3 a powerful tool for both health and convenience.

However, the watch does have its limitations. Repairability, for instance, is still a concern. Unlike some competitors, Google does not offer repair options for the Pixel Watch—damaged units are simply replaced rather than repaired. Additionally, while Google promises three years of software updates, this falls short compared to the seven years of support offered for its Pixel smartphones.

The Future of Google Wearables

Looking ahead, Google continues to explore ways to enhance its wearable offerings. While there’s speculation about the potential for other form factors, such as smart rings, Google appears content with its current lineup of smartwatches and fitness trackers, believing that these products already meet a wide range of user needs.

The Pixel Watch 3, priced from $349, is available for preorder now and will be released on September 10. It represents Google’s latest effort to establish itself as a leader in the wearable tech space, balancing cutting-edge innovation with the practical challenges of battery life and user trust.

Conclusion

The Google Pixel Watch 3 is a bold step forward in wearable health technology. With its life-saving pulse detection feature, Google is not just catching up to its competitors but also pushing the boundaries of what smartwatches can do. As the wearable market continues to evolve, the Pixel Watch 3 positions Google as a serious contender, offering both advanced health monitoring and a suite of smart features that make it a compelling choice for tech-savvy consumers

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Google is stepping up its game in the wearable tech market with the introduction of the Pixel Watch 3. This latest smartwatch is not just about telling time

item.title: Plaud Launches AI-Powered NotePin Voice Recorder: Preorder Now
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Plaud has introduced its latest AI wearable, the NotePin, an AI-powered voice recorder designed to boost productivity. Available for preorder in the U.S. at $169, NotePin is shaped like a pill and comes in three colors: Cosmic Gray, Lunar Silver, and Sunset Purple.

This versatile device can be worn as a pendant, pinned to your chest, or even worn as a wristband, making it convenient for recording notes and meetings. It offers basic AI features for free, while premium features like summary templates and speaker labeling are available for an annual subscription of $79.

Advanced AI Features

The NotePin allows users to choose between advanced AI models, including OpenAI’s GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet. These models enable the device to transcribe conversations, save notes in over 20 professional templates, and generate summaries.

Equipped with 64 GB of storage and a 270 mAh battery, NotePin also includes two MEMS microphones for clear audio capture. Each preorder includes an accessory kit, unlimited cloud storage, and a complimentary advanced AI membership.

Competing in the AI Wearable Market

Unlike other AI wearables like the Humane AI Pin, which faced negative reviews, Plaud’s NotePin focuses solely on productivity. It offers a straightforward set of features, positioning it as a reliable tool for professionals seeking an efficient voice recorder.

The NotePin’s blend of advanced AI functionality and practical design makes it a standout in the growing AI wearable market.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover Plaud's NotePin, the latest AI-powered voice recorder with customizable AI models. Preorder for $169 and get free accessories.

item.title: Nokia has emphatically rejected the recent rumors of interest by Samsung
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Nokia has emphatically rejected the recent rumors of interest by Samsung to acquire its mobile network business. The Finnish telecom giant said it remained committed to the strategic division.

Response to the Rumors - official

It added: "Nokia doesn't comment on speculation, and in relation to the article published today, there is nothing to announce and no such insider project exists." "Nokia is fully committed to the success of its mobile networks business, which is an essential strategic asset for both Nokia and our customers."
That follows a report from Bloomberg yesterday that suggested the Finnish company might be mulling various scenarios, including partial or an outright sale of the division. Changes are apparently still at an early stage, with the processes outlined by unnamed sources being sufficient to send Nokia stock higher by more than 7% on Thursday.

Progress Focus and Future Strategy

It also noted that recent progress at the mobile networks division involved cost reductions, protection of product roadmaps, and sealing new deals. These do form a background for a determination the company has to keep being competitive within its business of telecommunications, with continued global rollouts of the 5G networks.

Industry impact and future outlook

But the rumors, despite Nokia's stern denial, had generated enough interest in that segment of the industry. By various analyst estimates, Nokia's mobile networks unit can fetch a value as high as $10 billion, making the stakes extraordinarily high. As the telecom marketplace evolves, so can Nokia's strategy and structure alter the nature of the global marketplace. Yet, for now, Nokia seems quite dedicated to staying its existing course, in which it is focused on innovation and holding strong customer relationships. Analysts continue to watch with interest what Nokia will do next, but its latest statement certainly gives no indication of it selling out or merging anytime soon.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Nokia denies speculation about Samsung acquiring its mobile networks division, emphasizing commitment to innovation and customer relationships.

item.title: Wearable Devices Can Detect Early COVID-19, Heart Issues
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
According to a new study by the University of South Australia, smartwatches or fitness trackers can detect the illnesses of COVID-19 and heart conditions earlier.

Study Overview
It collated information from 1.2 million participants in 28 studies and found the devices correctly identified COVID-19 in 88% of cases - a similar result to rapid tests. They also detected the presence of atrial fibrillation - or an irregular heartbeat - in 87% of cases.

Fall and Cardiac Condition Detection
Apart from cases of COVID-19 and heart conditions, the devices detected fall correctly in 82% of cases. Results of this study have been published in the Journal of Medical Internet Research mHealth and uHealth and shed light on the ever-expanding role of wearable technology in personal health monitoring.

Beyond Fitness
Grown Although originally designed for tracking fitness, these wearables track heart rate, blood pressure, and quality of sleep, among other metrics. This makes one be in charge of his health and promptly act on problems.

Promising Future
Lead author Ben Singh said devices like these promise to improve health monitoring. "Water trackers not only help people become more engaged in their health care but also enable them to monitor their health in real-time," Singh said. Because of this, it has the potential—as wearable technology development continues—to enable the early detection of disease, which may reduce the severity of health problems simply through earlier intervention.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Wearable devices like smartwatches could detect early signs of COVID-19 and heart conditions, helping improve health monitoring, a University of South Australia study suggests

item.title: Predicting Migraines with Wearable Tech: New Insights from Sleep Data
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
A migraine is a severely painful incident with highly variable symptoms and impacts millions around the globe. Classic methods of prediction and management of migraine attacks are less effective because of the various complex triggers. Emerging wearable technologies are giving several monitoring opportunities for physiological changes in anticipation of a migraine attack.

Wearable Technology and the Prediction of Migraines

Wearables, such as the Empatica Embrace Plus, continuously monitor heart rate, skin temperature, and EDA. It is the abovementioned indicators that are used in showing the state of the body before an attack of migraine.
The present study takes advantage of this technology to drive predictive models of migraine onset using machine learning analysis of the data. Despite their relatively low accuracy, current models are a step forward in migraine management.

Study Overview: Data Collection and Data Analysis
Physiological data will be collected for ten migraine participants using Empatica Embrace Plus in this study. Many biomedical signals from EDA, skin temperature, heart rate, and activity level were considered in analyses that could be deployed in a study on the prediction of migraine attacks.

Some of these machine learning methods used within the study by the researchers are Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, and KNN. The participants investigated how varying time frames of analysis between 5 and 120 minutes in length could affect the predictive accuracy.

Key Findings: Better Outcomes from Shorter Time Frames

The best results, concerning F1-score and recall, achieved in the present work were performed only with frames of analysis of smaller lengths: 5 or 10 minutes. Smaller time slots may capture variations in physiological traits better before a migraine attack.
The results also presented some specific features like EDA, skin temperature, and activity counts for the prediction of a migraine. Traditional statistical metrics outperformed unusual features, hence speaking to the fact that focused feature extraction has an important role in predictive modeling.

Discussion: Challenges and Future Directions Though promising, the results obtained with this method included a small number of subjects and did not consider a group without migraine headaches as a control. It did not consider other potential triggers as well in predicting its accuracy, such as changes in weather or cycles of menstruation that might be associated with migraine attacks.

In future studies, sample pools should be expanded to cover more triggers and further diversified. Future efforts may be advanced into various machine-learning algorithms and into the use of comprehensive data to enhance the predictive accuracy made. Conclusion Wearable technology, along with machine learning, is a very promising way of working on the forecasting of migraine attacks. If the analysis frame is kept small and focused on some physiological features, then more appropriate results can be achieved. Certainly, there are challenges in this work, but further research in this area can really make a great contribution toward increased quality of life for sufferers. Refinement of predictive models and extending research will make really personalized, effective management strategies for migraine.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Wearable technology and machine learning models predict migraines by analyzing physiological signals during sleep. The potential for better migraine management.

item.title: Intel’s Core Ultra 200V: What's new?
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Introduction
Intel has just officially announced its lineup of Core Ultra 200V chips, also referred to as Lunar Lake. These are meant to go toe-to-toe against Qualcomm's recently announced Snapdragon X Elite and AMD's Strix Point chips. Intel says these new chips include the best of everything: the fastest CPU, the fastest GPU, and the best AI performance. But have they been worth waiting for?

Key Features of Intel's Core Ultra 200V Chips
The lineup of Core Ultra 200V introduces significant improvements to laptop processing power. Intel bills these chips as featuring "the fastest CPU core," "the world's best built-in GPU," and "the best AI performance." The laptops running these chips, like the Dell XPS 13, boast up to 26 hours of Netflix streaming. This represents a 44% increase over predecessors.

Performance Claims vs. Competitors
New Intel chips try to outcompete both Qualcomm and AMD in key departments. The chip Core Ultra 9 288V promises 68% better frame rates than Qualcomm's X1E-84-100 and 16% better than AMD's HX 370 when gaming at 1080p medium settings. That would put Intel finally ahead of AMD in some aspects of gaming, while Qualcomm lags.

Intel even boasts its chips grant batteries with longer lives in machines such as the Asus Zenbook S 14, beating larger-battery counterparts such as AMD's Asus Zenbook S 16.

Possible Flaws of Lunar Lake
But, among all the roar, there are a couple of limitations with these Core Ultra 200V chips: They're designed for thin laptops and also support only up to 32GB RAM, without the possibility of future upgrades. The chips don't feature hyperthreading either, which might affect their performance in multi-threaded tasks concerning older or more powerful chips, such as Intel's own Meteor Lake or the upcoming Arrow Lake.

Further, while Intel promises huge dividends in terms of efficiency and performance, this may be to a lesser degree depending on the power supply. High-watt tasks may still see better performance from AMD's HX 370 or Intel's Meteor Lake chips.

What to Expect and When
Laptops boasting Intel's new chips won't start shipping until September 24th. But would-be buyers may want to wait for in-depth reviews to appear before pulling the trigger. Past experience with both Qualcomm and AMD indicates these initial claims will often differ from actual real-world performance.

Conclusion: Should You Wait?
Yes, some exciting promises are certainly made with the Core Ultra 200V chips from Intel, but in reality, performance and value for money can only be told once independent reviews are in. More powerful options on the horizon, such as Arrow Lake, mean maximum performance buyers will appreciate holding off that little bit longer before pulling the trigger.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Intel's Core Ultra 200V chips promise top performance in CPU, GPU, and AI. But should you wait to buy? Here’s what you need to know before upgrading

item.title: Honor BookArt 14: Everything you need to know
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The recently unveiled creation by Honor at IFA 2024, the BookArt 14, drew attention for its detachable webcam. That is going to redefine the way laptop cameras are designed since users will be able to keep their screen clean and free from bezels while they can ensure maximum privacy. This magnetic webcam attached through pogo pins will fit onto the lid when needed for use and is just that easily stored out of sight when not in use

Sleek Design with Enhanced Connectivity
X Honor's BookArt 14 is no exception to the brand tradition of pushing limits further in sleek and minimalistic laptop design. It features a uniform 2mm bezel on three sides of its 14.6-inch display, which makes it quite sleek and enables the higher end display area. This is a 3.1K OLED display that shows great visuals, peaking at a brightness of 700 nits to ensure it will work great even in brighter conditions.

The port selection is designed for versatility on the laptop, offering dual USB-C ports, a USB-A port, HDMI-out, and a 3.5mm audio jack. With such a wide range of connectivity options, this makes the BookArt 14 ideal for professionals, students, and casual users alike who need to have the device connected to several peripherals.

Privacy and Practicality with the Detachable Webcam
But perhaps more interestingly, the detached webcam offers better privacy and flexibility with the Honor BookArt 14. In its idle position, the camera sits inside a recessed compartment at the right side of the deck. Just pop it out and attach the magnetic pogo pins at the top of the display when needed. This also allows users to easily flip the position of the camera, which can be especially useful for conference calls in large rooms or different setups.

Unlike other earlier models, which hide the webcams in the function row-resulting in an awkward angle during video calls-this new approach brings in a far better experience for the user. This kills the notorious "up-the-nose" view and offers a more natural position of the camera.

Power and Performance Under the Hood
The BookArt 14 runs on Intel's Core Ultra 7 for top-notch performance in mundane tasks down to the most resource-intensive applications. This means that whether into media editing, coding, or having many apps open, this laptop won't complain. It directly competes with the MacBook Air but does so with a considerably smaller size, better connectivity options, and higher concern for privacy, hence being in a good position to compete in the high-end laptop segment.

In other words, the Honor BookArt 14 is not only a beauty but also a powerful and privacy-conscious notebook, fit for stylish and pragmatic users.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Honor BookArt 14 debuts with a detachable webcam, sleek design, and a 14.6-inch OLED display, offering enhanced connectivity and privacy.

item.title: LG Display Unveils Stretchable Micro-LED for Wearable Tech
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
LG Display made waves at Seoul Fashion Week with its groundbreaking stretchable micro-LED technology. This cutting-edge innovation offers the ability to stretch, fold, and twist the display without damaging its functionality, a significant step forward for the future of wearable technology. By integrating this technology into clothing and accessories, LG is pushing the boundaries of fashion and tech.

Stretchable Displays on the Runway

LG Display’s new technology wasn’t just an announcement; it took center stage as part of the fashion show. The stretchable displays were incorporated into bags and clothing worn by models, allowing them to scrunch, twist, and stretch the displays live on the runway. This marked a stark departure from the original demo LG showcased in 2022, where the 12-inch micro-LED panel could be stretched up to 14 inches without damage. Now, it’s become a functional element of high-fashion design, blending seamlessly with wearable concepts.

Korean fashion designer Chung-Chung Lee, who collaborated with LG Display for this showcase, noted that the new technology could enable designs previously thought impossible. Stretchable displays could revolutionize the fashion industry by making interactive, tech-infused clothing more accessible.

A Game Changer for Wearable Tech

Although the stretchable display is still in its prototype stage, LG Display envisions a future where these screens could be attached to clothing and the skin. Such displays could be used for wearable tech, fitness tracking, or even interactive fashion statements. Imagine a lightweight screen integrated into your sleeve or stretched across your arm for real-time updates, notifications, or multimedia interaction.

One of the most intriguing possibilities is the potential to make skin-mounted displays. If LG can find a way to integrate power sources, such as batteries or energy-efficient designs, we could see truly wearable screens in everyday life. LG’s stretchable displays have the potential to become a new standard for wearable electronics, possibly used for health monitoring, interactive fashion, and entertainment on the go.

Commercialization and the Future of Stretchable Displays

While LG Display hasn’t yet shared full details on the resolution, size limitations, or power requirements for these stretchable screens, the progress they’ve made is undeniable. The company acknowledges that there are still technical challenges to overcome, but the fact that the display is functional on the runway signifies a big step forward.

LG is not alone in this field. Samsung has also developed stretchable displays, and Apple recently secured a patent for similar technology. This competition could accelerate the commercialization of stretchable displays, which could soon be integrated into everyday fashion and accessories, from t-shirts to handbags.

LG’s innovation represents a significant shift in how we think about displays. These aren’t just screens anymore; they’re becoming a part of what we wear, opening up new possibilities for fashion, fitness, health, and lifestyle technology. The future of wearable tech has never looked more flexible—literally.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: LG Display's stretchable micro-LED tech debuted at Seoul Fashion Week, revolutionizing wearable screens for clothing and accessories.

item.title: Apple Watch: A Tale of 10 years
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
A Look Back at the First Apple Watch: Apple's Entry into Wearables

2024 marks the 10th anniversary of the Apple Watch, Apple's foray into the wearable tech space. Announced on September 9, 2014, the Apple Watch introduced innovative features like the Digital Crown and Force Touch, changing the smartwatch landscape.

Conceptualization and Launch

Codenamed "Gizmo", Apple began developing the watch in 2011 under Jony Ive’s leadership. The goal? Create a device that revolutionized communication, health tracking, and user interface. After extensive R&D, Apple finally revealed its first wearable in 2014, with its release on April 24, 2015.

First Apple Watch Features and Specifications

The first Apple Watch came in 38mm and 42mm sizes and three collections: Sport, Stainless Steel, and the high-end Edition with an 18-karat gold case. Its Retina display featured Force Touch, and the Digital Crown allowed users to scroll and navigate without touching the screen.

Powered by the Apple S1 chip, it had 512 MB RAM and 8 GB storage. The watch tracked heart rate, steps, and calories, syncing with iPhone GPS for better accuracy.

Health and Connectivity

Although it offered basic health monitoring—heart rate, steps, and calories—the watch required an iPhone 5 or later for most functions. It featured Bluetooth 4.0, Wi-Fi, and an 18-hour battery life charged via MagSafe.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Explore how Apple entered the wearable space with the first Apple Watch, launched in 2014. Learn about its specs, features, and impact on tech history.

item.title: CG Mobile's BMW Cases for Samsung's Latest Foldables
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
CG Mobile Launches BMW Cases for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 & Flip 6

CG Mobile, a leader in premium smartphone accessories, has introduced BMW-inspired cases for the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 and Flip 6. These cases combine luxury and protection, with designs crafted from high-quality materials like genuine leather, metal, carbon fiber, and ABS.

Luxury Meets Protection

Designed with BMW's iconic aesthetics, each case features debossed wordmarks, embossed lines, and a metal BMW logo, reflecting the brand's automotive elegance. They offer a slim, form-fitting design that maintains the sleek look of your smartphone while providing an ergonomic grip.

Engineered for Ultimate Protection

The cases boast raised edges to protect against drops, scratches, and impact damage, ensuring your device stays secure. Easy access to ports, cameras, and buttons ensures functionality isn't compromised.

Available in Multiple Variants

Available in five distinct designs, including Tricolor Strips & Metal Logo and Big Metal Logo, these cases come in Black and Brown, with prices starting at INR 2,699. Find them at Tekkitake.com or Amazon.in.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: CG Mobile introduces BMW-inspired cases for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 & Flip 6. Luxurious protection with genuine leather and metal accents starting from INR 2,699.

item.title: UK Responds to Rise in Phone and Bag Thefts
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The British government has unveiled its 'Safer Streets Mission' in response to an alarming rise in street thefts, particularly involving mobile phones and bags. Over the past year, these crimes have surged by more than 150%, with around 78,000 victims across the UK. The spike is driven by growing demand for second-hand smartphones in both domestic and international markets.

Technology and Law Enforcement Collaboration

In an effort to crack down on these crimes, the UK government will partner with tech companies to explore advanced anti-theft features for smartphones. The Home Office plans to host a summit, where smartphone manufacturers will discuss innovations to make stolen devices permanently unusable. The focus is on enhancing existing security measures, preventing stolen phones from being re-registered or sold on the black market.

“Phone companies must ensure that stolen phones can be quickly disabled and not sold on the second-hand market,” said Dame Diana Johnson, UK Policing Minister. “We will be meeting with tech firms to discuss additional actions necessary to achieve this.” The initiative aims to curb the illegal trade and make it more difficult for thieves to profit from stolen smartphones.

Boost in Policing Efforts

Alongside technological solutions, the government is increasing police presence in areas most vulnerable to these crimes. The mission includes the deployment of thousands of additional uniformed officers to enhance neighborhood policing efforts. Local police forces will also intensify patrols in hotspots known for high theft rates, such as tourist-heavy areas of central London.

As part of this initiative, Operation Opal, a national police intelligence unit, will launch an intelligence probe to gather data on criminal networks involved in phone thefts. Operation Calibre, a targeted week of police action, has already been conducted in over 1,250 areas identified as robbery hotspots.

Theft Rates at Record Highs

New Home Office data indicates that personal robbery and theft from individuals have reached the highest rates in more than a decade. Crime statistics estimate over 200 incidents of snatch theft daily across England and Wales. Mobile phones were the target in more than one-third of these thefts.

The National Police Chiefs’ Council (NPCC) highlighted the devastating impact these crimes can have on victims, especially children and vulnerable individuals. "Personal robbery is traumatic for victims, especially when violence is threatened," said Commander Richard Smith, NPCC lead for Personal Robbery. "We’re committed to working with the government and tech companies to dismantle the business model driving these thefts."

Building a Safer Future

The UK government’s 'Safer Streets Mission' marks a significant step toward addressing the growing threat of street crime. By combining technological innovation with enhanced law enforcement, officials hope to restore a sense of safety to the streets and protect the public from the emotional and financial harm caused by theft.

With a collaborative effort between the government, police, and tech firms, the mission aims to make it harder for thieves to operate, ultimately reducing the number of phone and bag thefts in the UK.

This government-led mission is designed to protect citizens by both preventing theft and dismantling the illegal trade in stolen smartphones. With continued surveillance, patrols, and partnerships, the government hopes to reverse the rising trend of snatch thefts and restore public confidence in street safety.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The UK government launches 'Safer Streets Mission' to curb a 150% rise in phone and bag thefts, with over 78,000 snatch incidents in the last year.

item.title: Huawei Mate XT Tri-Fold, Everything you need to know
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Huawei latest innovation, the Mate XT tri-fold phone, has garnered over 2.7 million pre-orders, according to the company’s website on Monday. The Chinese tech giant opened pre-orders midday Saturday, more than two days before Apple's highly anticipated iPhone 16 launch.

Huawei Early Surge in Pre-Orders

The Mate XT will officially hit the market on September 20, but the rush of pre-orders suggests strong consumer interest. This marks Huawei's continued push in the foldable smartphone market, an area Apple has yet to enter. The new Mate XT features a three-fold display design, setting it apart from other models in the market.

A Competitive Landscape

Huawei's pre-order numbers come as Apple, which fell out of China's top five smartphone vendors in Q2 2024, prepares to unveil the iPhone 16. According to Canalys, Huawei ranked fourth in the Chinese market, shipping 10.6 million smartphones in the second quarter. Despite Apple’s dominance in the global smartphone market, it shipped fewer phones in China, trailing behind Huawei and other domestic competitors.

As the Mate XT prepares for its September 10 unveiling, the competition between Apple and Huawei is expected to heat up, particularly in the foldable phone segment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Huawei Mate XT tri-fold phone received over 2.7 million pre-orders, just days ahead of iPhone 16 release, marking fierce competition in the smartphone market.

item.title: Subtle Changes in Apple iPhone 16: What You Need to Know
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Spot the Subtle Differences in Apple's Latest iPhone 16 Line-Up

Apple's new iPhone 16 may look similar to last year's models, but it comes with some subtle yet impactful upgrades. A key new feature, the Camera Control button, sets the iPhone 16 apart. This small, slightly recessed groove is on all four versions and allows users to launch the camera instantly and fine-tune shots. Designed to mimic a DSLR shutter button, it enhances the photography experience, especially for right-handed users.

Larger Screens and Thin Borders

The Pro models now boast larger screen sizes—6.3 inches for the iPhone 16 Pro and 6.9 inches for the Pro Max—paired with the thinnest borders yet. However, these size increases are so subtle that many may not notice the difference compared to last year’s models.

Apple Intelligence and AI Features

More substantial changes will come via software updates, particularly with Apple Intelligence, a suite of AI tools launching in beta next month. These tools will focus on text summarization, email responses, and photo editing, offering features similar to Adobe and Google.

Camera and Siri Upgrades

For photography enthusiasts, the iPhone 16 Pro models feature a 48-megapixel ultrawide-angle camera, which is a major upgrade. Additionally, Siri has a new, refreshed interface that lights up around the border and can be activated with typed commands.

With prices starting at $799 for the base model and $1,199 for the Pro Max, preorders begin Friday, and shipping starts on September 20.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the subtle design and feature changes in Apple iPhone 16, including the new Camera Control button and upgraded AI tools.

item.title: Apple Rolls Out iOS 18 Release After iPhone 16 Launch
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Apple has rolled out the iOS 18 Release Candidate (RC) update, marking the final step before the official public release. This update follows the unveiling of the iPhone 16 series and is available to developers ahead of the September 16 public release.

iOS 18 Features and AI Tools

iOS 18 introduces advanced customization options for home and lock screens and Apple Intelligence—Apple's generative AI feature.
