item.title: Garmin Fenix 8 Series Launches in India for Athletes & Adventurers
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Garmin has introduced its latest Fenix 8 series in India, targeting athletes, fitness lovers, and outdoor adventurers. Built to withstand the elements, this advanced smartwatch offers a range of performance and health tools, making it the ultimate fitness partner.

Built for Tough Conditions

The Fenix 8 smartwatch’s design withstands tough environments with leakproof buttons, a metal sensor guard, and options for stainless steel or titanium bezels. Its durability meets U.S. military standards, ensuring it performs in extreme conditions. The 1.4-inch AMOLED display guarantees visibility, while a choice of silicone, leather, or metal straps allows for customization.

Advanced Training Tools

The Fenix 8 equips users with performance-focused tools for efficient and adaptable workouts:

  • Endurance Score: Combines VO2 max and training load for long-term performance insights.
  • Visual Race Predictor: Estimates race times for distances like 5Ks and marathons.
  • Training Readiness: Evaluates recovery and sleep quality for optimal workout recommendations.
  • PacePro & ClimbPro: Offers real-time GPS-based pace tracking and climb information.

These tools enable users to improve their performance safely without overtraining.

Outdoor Navigation

The Fenix 8 offers dependable multi-band GPS and detailed built-in maps, perfect for outdoor activities like hiking, skiing, and biking. Navigation features include:

  • NextFork Map Guide: Displays nearby trails and forks.
  • Altimeter, Barometer, and Compass: Delivers real-time environmental data.
  • SATIQ Technology: Manages GPS power use to maximize battery life.

With TopoActive maps and ski resort maps, users can navigate varied terrains worldwide.

Health Monitoring & Smart Features

Garmin includes comprehensive health-tracking tools:

  • Pulse Ox Sensor: Monitors blood oxygen levels, ideal for high-altitude activities.
  • HRV and Body Battery: Tracks stress, recovery, and daily energy levels.
  • Women’s Health Tracking: Supports menstrual and pregnancy tracking.

Smart features enhance connectivity, with phone calls, Garmin Pay, and music control. A built-in flashlight adds safety with adjustable brightness, including a red night light.

Long-Lasting Battery Life

With impressive battery longevity, the Fenix 8 is designed for extended use:

  • Smartwatch Mode: Up to 29 days (13 days with always-on display).
  • Max Battery GPS Mode: Up to 145 hours.
  • Expedition Mode: Extends battery life to 31 days.

Pricing and Availability

The Garmin Fenix 8 series is available now, starting at Rs. 86,990, with a two-year warranty. Purchase it at premium retail outlets or directly from the Garmin India website.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Explore the new Garmin Fenix 8 series smartwatches, designed for outdoor enthusiasts and athletes. Available now in India, starting at Rs. 86,990.

item.title: Nothing Launches New Ear (Open) Earbuds with Unique Open-Ear Design
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
British consumer tech startup Nothing is launching its latest wireless earbuds, the Nothing Ear (Open), today, September 24. According to leaked images from WinFuture, these earbuds bring a minimalist white design, a unique open-ear fit, and a curved band that wraps around the back of the ear.

Key Features and Design

Open-Ear Design: Unlike in-ear earbuds, the Nothing Ear (Open) provides an open-ear experience, allowing ambient sounds to mix naturally with audio playback. This design also means no Active Noise Cancellation but offers a comfortable, lightweight feel suitable for extended wear.

Audio and Drivers: Each earbud features a 14.2mm titanium-coated driver optimized with Nothing's Bass Enhance algorithm, aiming to deliver deep, clear audio without intrusive isolation.

Battery Life: Users can enjoy up to eight hours of playtime on the earbuds alone and up to 30 hours with the charging case. For phone calls, battery life extends up to six hours, with an additional 24 hours using the case.

Connectivity and Smart Features

Dual Device Support: Equipped with Bluetooth 5.3, the Nothing Ear (Open) can connect to multiple devices simultaneously, making it seamless to switch between your phone and laptop.

Touch Controls: The earbuds come with touch controls for easy playback, call handling, and switching between connected devices. Additionally, these earbuds are expected to support ChatGPT integration (when paired with a Nothing Phone), allowing users to interact with the AI assistant directly through the earbuds.

Durability: The earbuds and case are IP54 rated for splash and dust resistance, making them reliable for both indoor and outdoor use.

Conclusion

The Nothing Ear (Open) earbuds offer a fresh take on wireless audio with their open-ear design, signature transparent style, and AI-driven features. Their unique design, enhanced audio clarity, and multi-device connectivity make them a compelling option for tech enthusiasts looking for an alternative to traditional in-ear models.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Explore the Nothing Ear (Open) wireless earbuds with open-ear design, ChatGPT integration, and enhanced audio clarity.

item.title: Apple Enhances iPhone and Watch Functions with Advanced AI Technology
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Apple is using artificial intelligence to elevate the basic functions of its latest devices, from iPhones to the Series 9 Watch. Though Apple hasn’t spotlighted AI extensively, this new integration subtly but powerfully enhances user experiences in core areas like calls, photography, and device interaction.

Enhanced Functions and New Features

Apple’s Series 9 Watch now includes a new chip with a four-core Neural Engine, which accelerates machine learning tasks by twice the previous speed, according to Reuters. This improved processing power brings greater accuracy and efficiency to Siri, now 25% more accurate in responding to voice commands.

AI-driven enhancements also introduce a unique “double tap” gesture, allowing users to interact with their devices by simply pinching their fingers on the watch hand. This new input method can answer calls, pause music, or check the weather hands-free.

Improved Photography on iPhones

Apple’s latest iPhones offer enhanced image capture, improving the Portrait Mode that blurs backgrounds to mimic a large camera lens effect. This AI-driven feature allows users to take professional-looking photos with minimal effort, demonstrating how AI can improve mobile photography for day-to-day use.

Comparisons with Industry Rivals

While Apple has integrated AI enhancements, competitors like Google and Microsoft have set more extensive goals for AI across products. Google’s Pixel phones, for instance, offer AI-based image editing tools that can erase unwanted objects from photos. The cautious approach to AI seen from Apple contrasts with Google’s more experimental implementations but underscores Apple’s focus on stable and incremental upgrades.

Conclusion

With new AI-enabled features in its iPhones and Series 9 Watch, Apple shows how advanced technology can improve everyday functions without radically altering user experiences. Whether it’s a more responsive Siri, intuitive gesture controls, or enhanced photos, Apple’s subtle yet impactful AI integrations mark a new chapter for its devices.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Apple integrates AI to enhance iPhone and Watch functions, improving Siri’s accuracy, call handling, and photography. Discover Apple’s subtle yet impactful AI upgrades.

item.title: ‘Ki’ Standard to Bring Wireless Charging to Kitchen Appliances
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Wireless Power Consortium (WPC), known for its popular Qi wireless charging standard used in smartphones, is now introducing Ki, a new standard designed for small kitchen gadgets. Showcased at the IFA 2024 tech trade show in Berlin, Ki aims to make common kitchen appliances like blenders, kettles, and coffee makers completely cordless, revolutionizing countertop convenience. The standard uses magnetic inductive charging to provide up to 2.2 kilowatts of power, a setup similar to what Qi offers but for more power-intensive devices.

How Ki Works and What It Means for Kitchens

Ki’s magnetic induction charging allows a device to charge simply by placing it on a compatible charging surface, such as an induction cooktop. When positioned on these surfaces, Ki-enabled appliances communicate with the charging base through NFC (Near Field Communication) to ensure the correct power delivery. For busy kitchen spaces, this means fewer cords and more efficiency.

According to WPC’s whitepaper, Ki is ideal for small appliances like juicers, rice cookers, coffee makers, toasters, and air fryers. The technology is designed to deliver over 90% efficiency, comparable to traditional plug-in appliances. Blenders were among the first appliances displayed using Ki technology, likely because of their high power needs, making them perfect candidates to showcase the potential of cordless kitchen gadgets.

Ki Rollout Timeline and Market Availability

The first Ki-compatible induction cooktops and appliances are anticipated to launch in early 2025. Midea, a key WPC partner, confirmed its intention to integrate Ki technology into blenders, kettles, and air fryers. The cost to add a Ki receiver to an appliance varies between $8 and $10, depending on the power needs and coil size, making it feasible for a wide range of small appliances.

The Ki system has garnered support from major manufacturers like Bosch, Philips, and Midea, showing industry interest in a future with wireless-powered kitchens. A WPC database of Ki-certified devices is expected by the end of 2024, ensuring compatibility across brands and appliances.

Ki’s Potential for Future Kitchen Designs

In the second phase of Ki’s implementation, WPC aims to incorporate charging coils directly into countertops. These under-countertop chargers would allow any Ki-enabled device to be powered by placing it on the countertop, creating an even more seamless cooking experience. However, integrating these chargers below countertops poses technical challenges. The WPC is working to ensure that the Ki chargers can distinguish between compatible appliances and other metal objects like coins or utensils.

The addition of remote control capabilities via smartphone apps will be a later feature, allowing users to control appliances like coffee makers and slow cookers through their devices. This feature is expected to be especially beneficial in a connected smart home environment.

While adding a Ki-enabled charging system to an existing countertop may be costly, WPC estimates that its expense could be minimized when integrated into kitchen remodels, fitting easily within the budget of high-end renovations.

Conclusion

The Ki standard represents a significant innovation in making the kitchen a more streamlined and connected space. With cordless blenders, kettles, and other small appliances soon to be available, and cooktops capable of powering them wirelessly, the kitchen of the future is just around the corner. WPC’s introduction of Ki is likely to impact both appliance manufacturers and consumers, paving the way for a smarter, safer, and more efficient kitchen experience.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the Ki standard from the creators of Qi charging, set to enable cordless power for small kitchen gadgets like blenders and kettles. Coming soon to cooktops and countertops.

item.title: Cellecor Gadgets Expands Its Retail Presence Across South India
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Cellecor Gadgets Limited, one of India’s rapidly growing consumer electronics brands, has announced new retail partnerships across South India and Gujarat, broadening its reach to a wider customer base. The partnerships are a major step in Cellecor’s retail expansion strategy, focused on providing customers with an enhanced shopping experience and direct access to its diverse product range.

Expanding Across Telangana and Andhra Pradesh

Cellecor has partnered with Sangeetha Mobiles and Cellbay Mobiles and Electronics, two of South India’s top retail chains, to boost its footprint in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh. With Sangeetha’s 800+ stores and Cellbay’s fast-growing multi-brand presence of over 50 stores, Cellecor’s products—such as smart TVs, smartphones, smart gadgets, and home appliances—will be readily available, enabling customers to explore and purchase cutting-edge electronics with ease.

Strategic Partnerships in Gujarat

Cellecor’s expansion in Gujarat includes partnerships with key retailers such as PhoneWale Limited, Poojara Telecom Pvt Ltd, Umiya Mobile Pvt Ltd, and Ramdev Mobiles. These partnerships ensure Cellecor’s presence in both urban and semi-urban areas, with over 1,000 retail touchpoints. PhoneWale Limited and Poojara Telecom each operate over 500 stores, while Umiya Mobile and Ramdev Mobiles contribute 150 and 77 stores, respectively. This strong network is projected to drive Cellecor’s revenue in the region, aiming to surpass ₹100 crores within the next 12 months.

About Cellecor Gadgets Limited

Founded in 2012 by Ravi Agarwal, Cellecor Gadgets Limited began as Unity Communications and has since evolved into a renowned name in the consumer electronics sector. Known for its innovative yet affordable products, Cellecor’s range includes smartphones, smart TVs, audio devices, home appliances, and wearables. The company’s sustainable growth strategy combines quality, affordability, and market demand, striving to make happiness accessible to all through reliable and advanced electronic products.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Cellecor Gadgets announces partnerships with leading retail chains across South India and Gujarat, making top consumer electronics more accessible to Indian customers.

item.title: Cellecor Appoints Kareena Kapoor Khan as Brand Ambassador for Smart TVs
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Cellecor Gadgets Limited has announced Bollywood actress Kareena Kapoor Khan as the new brand ambassador for its Smart TV segment. This collaboration, aimed at reinforcing Cellecor's position in the smart TV market, aligns with the brand’s mission to reach a broader audience across India.

Kareena Kapoor’s association with Cellecor is part of an extensive advertising campaign featuring TV, digital, and offline media. Known for her strong influence in popular culture, Kareena’s endorsement is expected to boost the brand’s appeal and showcase the affordability and advanced features of Cellecor’s Smart TVs, such as ultra-HD displays, immersive sound, and multiple smart connectivity options.

Strategic Growth and Financial Highlights

Cellecor has reported impressive growth with a 103% increase in net sales to ₹425.71 crores in H1 FY25, alongside a 106% increase in profit before tax, now at ₹19.67 crores. This reflects a substantial upward trend from the previous fiscal year’s performance, showcasing Cellecor’s strengthening position in the consumer electronics market.

About Cellecor Gadgets Limited

Established in 2010, Cellecor Gadgets specializes in consumer electronics, including smart TVs, smartphones, smartwatches, and audio accessories. With a distribution network of over 900 distributors and 25,000 retailers across 28 states, Cellecor has a strong market presence, particularly in Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal, and Gujarat.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Bollywood star Kareena Kapoor Khan becomes the face of Cellecor's Smart TV range, aiming to elevate the brand's market presence with a nationwide ad campaign.

item.title: Xiaomi Watch S4 and Smart Band 9 Pro
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Xiaomi is preparing for a major launch event in China on October 29, featuring its flagship Xiaomi 15 series, Pad 7 tablet lineup, and HyperOS 2.0. As confirmed through teaser images that offer a first look at the wearables, the Xiaomi Watch S4 and Xiaomi Smart Band 9 Pro will join these launches.

Xiaomi Watch S4 and Smart Band 9 Pro: Key Features and Specifications

Launch Details:

  • Date: October 29
  • Time: 7:00 pm CST (4:30 pm IST)

Xiaomi Watch S4 Highlights

The Xiaomi Watch S4 features a circular display and is expected to come with interchangeable bezels, allowing users to customize the look. Running on the new HyperOS 2.0, it includes a new intercom mode. The Watch S4 is likely to succeed the Watch S3, enhancing the user experience with a brighter display and improved functionality.

Xiaomi Watch S4 Sport Features:

  • 1.43-inch AMOLED display with a 466 x 466 resolution
  • 15-day battery life
  • NFC and eSIM connectivity

Xiaomi Smart Band 9 Pro Details

The Xiaomi Smart Band 9 Pro will feature a rectangular AMOLED display and will launch with multiple strap color options like black, pink, and silver. The design is sleek and sporty, with enhanced brightness over the previous model.

Stay tuned for the launch event on October 29 to see how Xiaomi’s latest wearables compete in the expanding world of smart technology!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Xiaomi unveils the Watch S4 and Smart Band 9 Pro on October 29 alongside the Xiaomi 15 Series. Expect brighter displays, HyperOS 2.0, and interchangeable bezels.

item.title: Samsung One UI 7 Beta Expected to Arrive by Mid-November
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
While Google and Vivo have already released their Android 15 updates, Samsung Galaxy users can expect One UI 7 to arrive next year. However, a One UI 7 beta program is likely to launch sooner, giving users a first look at Samsung's newest features by mid-November.

According to Ice Universe, a well-known industry leaker, Samsung's first One UI 7 beta could arrive within two weeks. The update, reportedly ending in “XJW,” promises a range of enhancements and iPhone-inspired features.

What to Expect from One UI 7 on Samsung Galaxy Devices

iPhone-Inspired Design Changes

Leaks suggest One UI 7 may include iOS-like elements, including a dynamic island-style lock screen, a Control Center reminiscent of iOS, and smoother animations. These features are expected to refine the Samsung user experience, making it more fluid and visually appealing.

Galaxy AI and Notification Summary

In an exciting new feature inspired by Apple’s Notification Summary, Samsung’s “AI notification” will condense notifications when the screen is crowded, available initially in Korean but likely expanding to other languages soon. However, it won’t roll out on mid-range Galaxy A-series devices.

One UI 7 Beta Timeline

The delayed beta launch marks a shift from Samsung’s usual timeline, but with significant updates like Galaxy AI and iPhone-inspired design improvements, the wait may be worth it. Galaxy S25 series users can look forward to the stable version next year, with the beta program giving Galaxy owners a preview of these major updates before 2024 ends.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Samsung Galaxy users may get their first look at One UI 7’s Android 15 beta by mid-November, bringing iPhone-inspired features and AI enhancements.

item.title: Xiaomi Watch S4 Debuts with Interchangeable Bezels
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Xiaomi has introduced the Xiaomi Watch S4, a versatile new smartwatch packed with personalization options and advanced health tracking features. Announced alongside the Xiaomi 15 series, the Watch S4 offers an affordable alternative to the Watch S4 Sport and introduces customizable bezels, expanding style options for users.

Key Features and Specifications

  • Customization: The Watch S4 offers interchangeable straps, watch faces, and replaceable bezels, allowing users to adapt their look easily.
  • Display: 1.43-inch AMOLED screen with 1,500 nits peak brightness for enhanced outdoor visibility.
  • Gesture Control: Users can activate functions like smart home control with a flick of the wrist, adding convenience.
  • Health and Fitness: Equipped with Goodix Technology’s sensor, the Watch S4 provides 98.2% accurate heart rate and blood oxygen tracking, improved sleep monitoring, and access to 150+ sports modes.
  • Battery Life: Estimated 15-day battery life, dropping to 5 days with always-on display enabled.
  • Connectivity: NFC for contactless payments, optional eSIM, and GPS for fitness tracking.

Pricing and Availability

The Xiaomi Watch S4 is available in China starting at 999 yuan (€129), with the LTE eSIM variant priced at 1,199 yuan (€155). Global availability details have yet to be announced.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Xiaomi unveils the Watch S4 with customizable bezels, gesture control, and health tracking. Priced from 999 yuan, it offers advanced features at an affordable rate.

item.title: MIT Researchers Create Cell-Wearable Devices to Treat Neurological Disorders
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Scientists at MIT have introduced revolutionary cell-wearable devices designed to improve the treatment of neurological diseases such as multiple sclerosis (MS). These tiny, battery-free devices wrap around individual neurons, acting as synthetic myelin to restore disrupted electrical signaling caused by neurodegenerative diseases.

Synthetic Myelin for Damaged Axons

Crafted from soft polymers, these microdevices envelop neuronal structures without causing harm. Activated by specific light wavelengths, they adhere to axons and dendrites, imitating natural myelin. Deblina Sarkar, head of MIT’s Nano-Cybernetic Biotrek Lab, explains that this technology enables “intimate interfaces with neurons” for potential restoration of neural functions in damaged areas.

Advances in Microelectronics

The devices are made using azobenzene, a light-sensitive material that forms microtubes when exposed to specific light. Marta J. I. Airaghi Leccardi, a lead researcher, emphasizes that the fabrication process is scalable, making it feasible to mass-produce these cell-wearables for therapeutic purposes.

Future Applications

MIT researchers foresee integrating these devices with sensors to non-invasively monitor electrical and thermal signals in the brain. Flavia Vitale of the University of Pennsylvania hails this research as a promising foundation for developing new treatments for neurodegenerative diseases, potentially aiding in real-time monitoring and therapy.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: MIT researchers unveil cell-wearable devices that mimic natural myelin, potentially transforming treatments for neurological disorders like multiple sclerosis.

item.title: India Wearables Market has seen a slowdown over the past year
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
India's wearables market—including smartwatches and wireless audio devices—has slowed down over the past year. According to IDC, wearables shipments dropped by 10% year-over-year in the April-June quarter of 2024 due to cautious inventory management and fewer product launches. The festive season, typically a peak sales period, didn’t reverse the trend, especially in the mass-segment smartwatch category.

Boat and Boult Financial Overview

Despite a 5% drop in revenue to ₹3,122 crore in FY24, Boat, India’s leading wearables brand, halved its net losses, pulling out of the smartwatch segment to focus on audio products. Meanwhile, Boult achieved a 40% revenue increase to nearly ₹700 crore but saw profits shrink as the company expanded into smartwatches and offline retail.

Shift in Consumer Behavior and Product Preferences

Senior analyst Anshika Jain from Counterpoint Research notes that consumer demand for high-end smartwatches from brands like Samsung and Apple is rising, while budget smartwatches have seen limited replacement cycles and price-sensitive consumers.

In contrast, wireless audio remains resilient. Boult’s true wireless audio category grew over 25% in the last year, bolstered by quick commerce and a strong offline presence in over 2,000 stores. Jain anticipates a doubling of wireless audio shipments via quick commerce in the coming months.

Future Trends in Wearables

While smartwatches face inventory buildup and a predicted 30% shipment decline this September quarter, audio wearables are gaining popularity, with affordable features like active noise cancellation now available in products priced under ₹2,000.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: India wearables market continues to decline despite the festive season, impacting brands like Boat and Boult as smartwatch sales drop and wireless audio grows.

item.title: Honor Watch 5 Launches in China with GPS, Advanced Health Monitoring, and 15-Day Battery Life
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Honor has unveiled the Honor Watch 5 in China, expanding its smartwatch lineup with new health and fitness features. Initially launched globally earlier this year, the Watch 5 offers a curved 1.85-inch AMOLED display with a 60Hz refresh rate and 450 × 390 resolution, delivering clear visuals for fitness tracking and daily usage.

Key Specifications and Features

  • Design and Display: Curved square 1.85-inch AMOLED display with a 60Hz refresh rate, aluminum alloy front, and polymer fiber back case weighing just 35g.
  • GPS and Positioning: Features the Honor Polaris positioning system with GPS support for BeiDou, GLONASS, Galileo, and QZSS. A large-area antenna ensures accurate tracking, even in dense cityscapes.
  • Health Monitoring: Tracks heart rate, blood oxygen levels, stress, and sleep. It includes advanced heart monitoring and one-touch health assessments.
  • Connectivity and Battery Life: Supports eSIM, LTE, Bluetooth 5.2, NFC, and is IP68 rated for water and dust resistance. The 480mAh battery lasts up to 15 days in Bluetooth mode or 10 days with eSIM.
  • Sports and Fitness: Includes 97 sports modes, an AI fat reduction assistant, and over 5,000 watch faces, with some interactive features unlocked by steps.

Pricing and Availability

The Honor Watch 5 is available in Morning Gold, Obsidian Black (999 yuan, $140) and Pine Green, Moonlight Gray, Snow White (1,199 yuan, $169). It’s available on Honor’s official website and select Chinese retailers.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Honor Watch 5 debuts in China with a 1.85-inch AMOLED display, standalone GPS, and advanced health monitoring. Starting at 999 yuan ($140).

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 7 Series Users Report Frustrating Phantom Notifications
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Samsung Galaxy Watch 7 and Galaxy Watch 7 Ultra are receiving negative feedback from users reporting a persistent "phantom notification" issue. Users report random vibrations without any accompanying notifications or screen activity, which has been affecting the Watch 7 series for weeks, as documented in Samsung’s community forums.

The Phantom Notification Issue

Users describe experiencing unexplained vibrations: one short shake, a pause, followed by three additional vibrations. Many report that the watch doesn’t display any notifications or light up, leading to speculation about a range of possible causes:

  • Google Maps Mirroring: Some users initially suspected Google Maps mirroring, though disabling it hasn't resolved the issue for everyone.
  • Samsung Health Platform Permissions: In certain cases, the vibrations stopped after permissions were re-enabled automatically for the Health Platform.
  • Galaxy S24 Connection: Some believe the issue may stem from the link between the Galaxy Watch 7 and Galaxy S24 smartphones, though this remains speculative.

Other Reported Issues

In addition to phantom vibrations, users also report battery-draining issues, which may or may not be related to the same problem. Samsung's BioActive Sensor on the Watch 7, designed to offer more accurate health data, has sparked interest but may contribute to these ongoing issues.

Samsung is aware of the issue but has yet to provide an official solution. In the meantime, users continue to share potential workarounds on forums as Samsung investigates the underlying cause.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Samsung Galaxy Watch 7 Ultra and Watch 7 users report "phantom notifications" causing vibrations without notifications, linked to battery drain and potential software issues.

item.title: Sustainable Soft Materials Could Transform Wearable Tech & Medical Devices
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Researchers from Northwestern University have introduced a new class of soft, eco-friendly material with the potential to replace plastics in wearable technology and medical devices. Designed with peptides and synthetic polymers, these materials are biodegradable and capable of holding electric charges like a battery, opening pathways for low-power energy storage and memory devices.

What Makes This Material Unique?

Developed from peptide amphiphiles—molecules that self-assemble in water—the new material integrates polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF), a plastic known for its ability to produce electrical charges under pressure (piezoelectricity). The result is a flexible structure that mirrors the electroactive properties of PVDF but with enhanced stability and environmental benefits.

Key Features

  • Flexible and Lightweight: These nano-sized ribbons conform to organic structures, potentially allowing for softer, embedded tech such as bioactive implants.
  • Energy Efficient: They hold charge with the lowest power ever recorded for multiaxial soft ferroelectrics, crucial for wearable tech.
  • Environmentally Friendly: Unlike conventional plastics, the materials are biodegradable and produce no toxic waste in the process.

Applications in Technology and Healthcare

With further development, this material could be used for micro-sized memory chips, energy storage units, and smart fabrics. It may also bring about softer medical implants with wireless activation to improve organ function, including applications in neural interfaces and regenerative medicine.

The Stupp Laboratory plans to further explore uses in wearable tech and renewable energy processes, aiming to advance applications in a more sustainable and biologically compatible direction.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Northwestern University’s soft, eco-friendly material mimics myelin and offers energy-efficient, flexible solutions for wearable tech and medical implants.

item.title: Anker Prime 20,000mAh Power Bank Review: Reliable Power for All Your Devices
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Anker Prime 20,000mAh power bank is a powerhouse for anyone juggling multiple devices on the go. With fast-charging capabilities, multiple ports, and a smart display, it’s changing how users experience portable power. Here’s a deep dive into what makes it a must-have.

Port of Call: Design & Features

The Anker Prime stands out with its sleek, durable design and built-in screen, providing crucial details like charging speed and battery status. Its 20,000mAh capacity can fully charge devices like a MacBook Air or iPhone 15 multiple times. You can charge up to three devices simultaneously with two USB-C ports supporting 100W each and one USB-A port at 65W.

Screen Insight: The screen displays battery life, time remaining, and charging speed, offering more than the typical indicator.

Charging Efficiency: A quick charge to full in about 1 hour 15 minutes keeps it ready when you are.

Performance & Testing

  • Multiple Device Compatibility: Easily powers laptops, phones, and cameras.
  • Battery Capacity: From zero, it topped up a MacBook Air in under 40 minutes and had juice left.
  • Low Phantom Drain: Holds charge effectively with minimal self-discharge.
  • Durable Build: Although slightly heavy (550g), it’s reliable, with Anker’s included felt pouch ensuring safe storage.

Downsides: Weight and Heat

At 5 inches long and 550g, it's best suited for backpacks rather than pockets. Heavy usage, especially outdoors, may also cause overheating, leading to automatic shutdown.

Price Point: Priced at Dh369, it offers significant peace of mind and power security for those constantly on the go.

Conclusion

The Anker Prime 20,000mAh power bank isn’t just a power source; it’s a power solution. Ideal for travelers and remote workers, it’s a worthy investment for reliable, robust power.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the Anker Prime 20,000mAh power bank with 200W capacity, fast charging, and a smart display. Perfect for those who need reliable power on the go.

item.title: Leica Unveils Sofort 2 Hybrid: Instant Print Camera
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Leica, the renowned photography brand trusted by professionals and hobbyists alike, has expanded its lineup with the launch of the Sofort 2 Hybrid instant camera. Designed to capture the nostalgia of instant print photography while delivering high-resolution digital images, the Leica Sofort 2 Hybrid combines the best of both worlds in one stylish and portable device.

The Sofort 2 Hybrid camera features a high-performance Leica Summar 1:2/2,4mm lens, allowing users to capture detailed, vibrant images instantly. Known for its durable build and streamlined design, the camera focuses on simplicity, making it easy to point, shoot, and print high-quality photos within seconds. This release marks Leica’s entry into the growing instant print camera market, bringing premium quality to on-the-go photography.

Key Features of the Leica Sofort 2 Hybrid Camera

  • Leica Summar Lens: The advanced Summar 1:2/2,4mm lens offers clarity and precision, ensuring every shot is high-quality.
  • Instant Printing Technology: Enjoy the immediacy of printing photos on demand, perfect for scrapbooking or keepsakes.
  • Compact, Durable Design: Leica’s Sofort 2 Hybrid is designed for portability, making it ideal for travel and outdoor photography.
  • Digital-Analog Fusion: Capture moments digitally and print only your favorite shots, combining the best of digital storage and instant printing.

Leica's Sofort 2 Hybrid camera appeals to users who want to experience the tangible satisfaction of printed photos without sacrificing image quality. Available now at Selfridges and select retailers, the Sofort 2 Hybrid is expected to be a popular choice this season among photography enthusiasts.

Pricing and Availability

Priced competitively for an instant-print digital camera, the Leica Sofort 2 Hybrid is available at major retailers, including Selfridges, with potential expansion to additional global outlets soon.

This launch reflects Leica’s commitment to adapting its legendary quality to the latest photography trends, aiming to give users a seamless blend of classic and contemporary photography experiences.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the new Leica Sofort 2 Hybrid instant camera, blending high-quality digital photography with instant prints, perfect for professionals and hobbyists.

item.title: Joy Resolve Portable Bean Grinder: Fresh Coffee Anytime, Anywhere
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Imagine being in the middle of an adventure—be it in the rugged terrain of British Columbia, the scenic foothills of Peru, or even a remote Airbnb with no café in sight. For coffee aficionados, instant coffee just won’t cut it. Enter the Joy Resolve Portable Bean Grinder, a compact yet powerful solution for freshly ground coffee wherever you are.

Key Features of the Joy Resolve Portable Bean Grinder

  • Sleek Aluminum Design: The grinder’s sturdy aluminum cylinder ensures durability and a premium feel, while its lightweight design makes it easy to pack and carry.
  • Precision Grinding: With adjustable grind settings, you can choose the perfect texture, from coarse to fine, to suit your preferred brew.
  • Adventure-Ready: Designed for those who value fresh coffee on the go, this portable grinder is perfect for travel, camping, or simply brewing on a busy day.

The Joy Resolve grinder is the ultimate tool for coffee enthusiasts who want to savor the authentic taste of freshly ground beans, no matter where they find themselves.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover Joy Resolve’s new portable bean grinder, perfect for coffee lovers seeking freshly ground beans anytime, anywhere. Now available at Selfridges.

item.title: Hövding 3: Revolutionary Airbag for Urban Cyclists
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Cyclists looking for safety without compromising style can turn to the Hövding 3 Urban Cyclist Airbag. Worn around the neck like a collar, this smart device uses advanced motion sensors to monitor your movements 200 times per second, instantly inflating around your head during an accident. Offering more than traditional helmets, the Hövding 3 provides a sleek, unobtrusive way to ensure safety on busy city streets.

Key Features of Hövding 3

  • Innovative Airbag Design: Unlike helmets, it inflates only upon detecting an impact, offering 8x better protection than standard bike helmets.
  • High-Tech Monitoring: With 200 motion readings per second, the Hövding 3 is engineered for precision and reliability in accident detection.
  • Comfort & Style: Worn as a collar, it combines style with comfort, making it ideal for city commuters.

Whether you're a daily city cyclist or enjoy occasional rides, the Hövding 3 is a blend of safety and style you won’t want to cycle without.

item.title: Boost Your Workflow with GigaDrive Super-Fast External SSD
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
GigaDrive Super-Fast External SSD: High-Speed Storage Built for Durability

The GigaDrive Super-Fast External SSD is designed to boost productivity with ultra-fast data transfer speeds reaching up to 2,800 MB/s. Available in 1 TB, 2 TB, and 4 TB sizes, this SSD caters to diverse storage needs, whether you’re managing large projects or backing up extensive files. Its rugged, waterproof design ensures it withstands the elements, and the built-in heatsinks keep it cool during intense use, while its 10-foot drop resistance offers peace of mind for on-the-go professionals.

Key Features of GigaDrive SSD

  • Lightning-Fast Transfer Speeds: Achieve up to 2,800 MB/s, making large file transfers quick and efficient.
  • Durability and Protection: Fully waterproof and built to survive drops, ideal for fieldwork and travel.
  • Efficient Heat Management: Internal heatsinks prevent overheating during intensive tasks.

Whether for professional or personal use, the GigaDrive Super-Fast SSD brings reliability and speed to any workflow.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: GigaDrive Super-Fast External SSD offers speeds up to 2,800 MB/s, waterproof durability, and storage options up to 4 TB. Perfect for productivity on the go.

item.title: Genki Wave Music Ring: Revolutionize Music Creation with Gestures
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Genki Wave Music Ring brings a whole new level of innovation to music production and performance. This wearable gadget allows you to control music using simple gestures like tapping, tilting, and rolling, making it perfect for composers, producers, and performers looking to enhance their creative process.

With customizable settings for sensitivity and motion range, the Genki Wave Music Ring adapts to your personal style, providing a seamless and intuitive music experience. Whether you’re creating complex compositions or performing live, this gadget offers a fresh, hands-on way to interact with your music.

Key Features of the Genki Wave Music Ring

  • Gesture-Based Control: Tap, tilt, and roll your way through music creation.
  • Highly Customizable: Adjust range, sensitivity, and more for a personalized experience.
  • Perfect for Performers and Composers: Enhance creativity and flow during live performances or composition sessions.

The Genki Wave Music Ring is a must-have tool for anyone looking to push the boundaries of their musical creativity.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Genki Wave Music Ring lets you control music with simple hand gestures, offering a creative and intuitive way to compose and perform.

item.title: Dyson V15 Detect Cordless Vacuum: Ultimate Dust Detection for Spotless Floors
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Dyson V15 Detect Cordless Vacuum: Smart Cleaning with Laser Dust Detection

The Dyson V15 Detect Cordless Vacuum redefines home cleaning with its advanced Laser Dust Detection technology. This feature utilizes an angled laser in the cleaner head to reveal dust particles you might otherwise miss, ensuring that every sweep leaves your floors spotless.

With a built-in sensor, the Dyson V15 also automatically adjusts suction power based on the dust detected, optimizing energy usage and providing powerful cleaning only when needed. It’s ideal for those seeking effortless and effective cleaning for dust-free floors.

Key Features of the Dyson V15 Detect Cordless Vacuum

  • Laser Dust Detection: See dust particles illuminated for a more thorough clean.
  • Automatic Power Adjustment: Smart sensor increases suction on dusty areas.
  • Cordless and Lightweight: Enjoy easy, convenient cleaning without tangling cords.

Upgrade to a tech-savvy cleaning experience with the Dyson V15 Detect, the ultimate solution for maintaining pristine, dust-free floors.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the Dyson V15 Detect Cordless Vacuum with Laser Dust Detection and automatic power adjustment for thorough, dust-free cleaning.

item.title: Taopatch Wellness Device: Light Therapy for Pain & Anxiety Relief
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Taopatch Wellness Device: Innovative Relief for Anxiety and Chronic Pain

The Taopatch Wellness Device blends light therapy and acupuncture to offer relief from anxiety and chronic pain. Using body heat to convert into therapeutic wavelengths, Taopatch enhances communication between your body and the central nervous system. This device promotes improved posture and a boost in mental and emotional well-being. Ideal for those seeking natural wellness solutions, Taopatch is an easy addition to any wellness routine.

Key Benefits of Taopatch

  • Dual Therapy: Combines light therapy with acupuncture benefits.
  • Supports Nervous System: Improves neural communication to relieve anxiety and chronic pain.
  • Posture and Well-Being: Encourages better posture and emotional wellness.

If you're seeking a non-invasive way to boost well-being and reduce discomfort, Taopatch could be the holistic solution for you.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the Taopatch Wellness Device, combining light therapy and acupuncture for anxiety, chronic pain, and posture improvement.

item.title: MEAZOR Digital Measuring Tool: Your All-in-One Solution for Precision Measurements
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The MEAZOR Digital Measuring Tool is an incredibly handy gadget for homeowners, architects, and DIY enthusiasts alike. With this smart device, you can create a 2D floor plan of any room simply by rotating it 360 degrees. Ideal for precise measurements, MEAZOR also functions as a scale, rolling ruler, level, angle measurer, and space measurer.

Whether you're planning a renovation or need exact measurements for a new project, this tool saves you time and ensures accuracy. The compact, all-in-one design lets you measure anything effortlessly, making it one of those essential gadgets you never knew you needed.

Key Features of the MEAZOR Digital Measuring Tool

  • 2D Floor Plan Generation: Rotate 360 degrees to capture entire room layouts.
  • Multi-Functionality: Includes a scale, rolling ruler, level, angle measure, and space measure.
  • Portable and User-Friendly: Compact and easy to use, perfect for professionals and DIYers alike.

Get the MEAZOR Digital Measuring Tool and make every measurement accurate and easy.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The MEAZOR Digital Measuring Tool simplifies measurements. This versatile gadget allows you to create 2D floor plans and measure dimensions effortlessly.

Key Features of HoomBand

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Drift into sleep effortlessly with the HoomBand Audio Bedtime Headband, offering guided meditations, stories, and white noise.

item.title: Apple iOS 18.2 Public Beta 1: New AI Features and Siri Updates on Select iPhones
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Apple iOS 18.2 Public Beta 1: New AI Features and Siri Updates on Select iPhones

Apple has released iOS 18.2 Public Beta 1, introducing innovative Apple Intelligence features and updates like Genmoji, Visual Intelligence, and ChatGPT-integrated Siri. Available since November 6, the beta version is open to developers and beta testers on iPhone 15 Pro, Pro Max, and iPhone 16 models. However, all iPhones get a preview of upcoming Mail and Find My enhancements. Here’s what’s new:

Key Features in iOS 18.2 Public Beta 1

  1. Genmoji: With this AI-driven emoji creator, users can describe a desired emoji, and their iPhone will generate new options based on those details. Adding these Genmojis to your keyboard brings a unique touch to your chats.
  2. Visual Intelligence: Apple's new answer to Google Lens, this feature uses AI to identify objects, translate text, and scan QR codes. For now, it's available on iPhone 16 models, allowing users to access it with a single press of the new camera control button.
  3. Image Playground: This creative tool lets users generate AI-based images through prompts in the standalone app or Messages. It also allows the inclusion of personal photos, adding a new dimension to visual expression.
  4. Siri and ChatGPT Integration: iOS 18.2 introduces a “glowing” Siri interface, enhanced by ChatGPT capabilities. Users can now use Siri with ChatGPT to perform detailed queries or get custom responses. Apple reminds users to verify critical information generated by ChatGPT responses.
  5. Upgrades to Mail: Mail now sorts emails into categories, similar to Gmail, to better organize important messages from ads and promotions. However, users can toggle back to the original view as needed.
  6. Find My Enhancements: Share lost item locations or display contact information, making it easier for others to return lost items to you.

While iOS 18.2’s final release date remains unconfirmed, these beta features mark a new era of personalized and AI-powered experiences for Apple users.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: OS 18.2 Public Beta 1 is now live, bringing Apple Intelligence-powered Genmoji, ChatGPT-integrated Siri, and Visual Intelligence to select iPhones.

item.title: Samsung One UI is Here and It can be seen in Galaxy Book before the Beta Release
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Samsung highly anticipated One UI 7.0 update, set for beta release soon, is already making waves as its app icons have appeared on Galaxy Book laptops. This unexpected preview came via the Galaxy Book Experience app update to version 3.4.0.0, which showcases new app icons for One UI 7.0, including refreshed icons for Phone, Samsung Notes, SmartThings, and Tips.

Galaxy Book Experience App Reveals New One UI 7.0 Icons
The update aligns with Samsung’s push to unify the One UI design across devices, from smartphones to smart home appliances and PCs. Among the new icons, Samsung Notes and the Tips app feature subtle redesigns, with the latter adopting a lighter yellow tone and a simplified icon style.

One UI 7.0 Beta Program Nears Launch
Samsung has indicated that the One UI 7.0 beta will start by mid-November, likely in key markets including India, Europe, the US, and Korea. The beta will initially target the Galaxy S24 series and other recent flagship devices, with stable releases expected early next year following the Galaxy S25 launch.

With updates aimed at making One UI the universal interface for Samsung’s ecosystem, the upcoming One UI 7.0 marks a significant step toward a cohesive user experience across Samsung’s product lineup.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Samsung Galaxy Book Experience app update reveals One UI 7.0 app icons, giving users an early look at the design for upcoming Galaxy devices.

item.title: The Indian government to reduce import limits on laptops, tablets, and PCs by 5%
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Indian government is gearing up to reduce import limits on laptops, tablets, and PCs by 5% annually starting in 2025. This phased reduction encourages domestic manufacturing under the Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, promoting self-reliance and reducing dependence on imports, particularly from China and Hong Kong, which supply nearly 70% of these items.

Extension for Free Imports until December 2024

Initially, import restrictions on IT hardware were planned for September 2024, but the deadline was extended until December 31, 2024. The new system mandates that import limits will use 2024–25 data as the base, with a 5% decrease each subsequent year to support a smoother transition to local production.

Industry Concerns and Upcoming Guidelines

The IT sector, valued at approximately $10 billion, has expressed concerns over possible supply disruptions. Tech giants like IBM, Dell, and Lenovo participated in consultations with government officials, seeking clarity on the new rules. An online import management system is expected to streamline the import process, reducing reliance on manual customs procedures.

Security and Self-Reliance Goals

The policy shift also aligns with national security interests by lowering dependence on specific geographies, particularly China. The government views this as a pivotal move to increase domestic production, reduce security risks, and establish India as a major player in the global IT hardware sector.

The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) will soon release further guidelines for the new import authorisation process, expected to impact India’s IT import landscape starting January 1, 2025.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: To promote domestic manufacturing, India plans a 5% annual reduction in import limits on laptops, tablets, and PCs starting from 2025, supporting the PLI scheme's local production targets.

item.title: Huawei Watch GT 5 Pro: A Detailed Review
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
The Huawei Watch GT 5 Pro introduces cutting-edge technology, building on its predecessors with powerful health-tracking, durable design, and advanced smart features.

Design & Build

The Watch GT 5 Pro is available in 46mm and 42mm models, with the titanium alloy body adding to its premium feel. Weighing 53 grams without the strap, it’s slightly bulkier than prior models, yet it still maintains comfort. Huawei includes a high-quality titanium strap, though users can easily swap it for standard 22mm options.

TruSense Health Monitoring

Huawei debuts its TruSense system in this watch, offering detailed heart rate, SpO2, blood pressure, and sleep monitoring. This six-sensor system was designed in collaboration with medical experts, and our tests showed accurate tracking comparable to medical-grade devices.

Enhanced Software with HarmonyOS 5.0

The watch runs on HarmonyOS 5.0, featuring interactive notifications, a QWERTY keyboard, and support for GPX track imports for runners. Petal Maps brings offline navigation, though it lacks detailed hill gradients. HarmonyOS updates expected in 2025 could add further refinements.

Battery Life

The GT 5 Pro excels in battery life. Under typical usage, it lasts up to 10 days. For athletes, GPS usage during a half-marathon drained only 5%, while a full 10-hour ultramarathon consumed minimal power. However, Always-On Display (AOD) reduces battery life to about 4 days.

Charging is flexible with Qi compatibility, though the included magnetic charger is recommended for stability.

Verdict

The Huawei Watch GT 5 Pro is a significant investment but offers top-notch performance for active and style-conscious users alike. The fluoroelastomer strap option offers a more affordable entry without compromising essential features.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Huawei Watch GT 5 Pro impresses with TruSense health, HarmonyOS 5.0, and robust battery life, making it ideal for active users and style seekers.

item.title: UVA Student Develops Game-Changing Wearable Navigation System
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
University of Virginia doctoral student Giovanna Camacho is breaking new ground with a wearable navigation device designed to aid search and rescue divers, assist soldiers, and potentially support the visually impaired. Inspired by her background in engineering psychology and diving, Camacho is developing a low-cost device that could improve underwater navigation in zero-visibility environments.

The system uses vibrating "tactors" embedded in a wearable belt, guiding users via precise, intuitive pulses rather than traditional rope tugs or verbal cues. Integrating GPS, the device offers silent guidance for divers, and future prototypes could serve additional applications, from military missions to assistive technology for the visually impaired.

With initial testing underway and funding from the Virginia Space Consortium and UVA, Camacho is working on a wireless, waterproof second prototype, and aims to integrate AI for enhanced movement prediction and user guidance.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: UVA doctoral student Giovanna Camacho creates an innovative wearable navigation system to enhance search and rescue, aid soldiers, and assist the visually impaired.

item.title: Mudra Link by Wearable Devices Honored with CES 2025 Innovation Award
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Yokneam Illit, Israel, November 14, 2024 – Wearable Devices Ltd. (Nasdaq: WLDS), a leader in AI-driven wearable technology, proudly announced that its Mudra Link neural interface wristband received the prestigious CES 2025 Innovation Award in the XR Technologies & Accessories category. This recognition highlights Mudra Link’s transformative capabilities, allowing hands-free control on Android, iOS, and other platforms using neural signals from subtle finger and wrist movements.

The Mudra Link is the latest from Wearable Devices, known for the Mudra Band, a former “Best of CES” winner. Mudra Link enhances user interaction in digital and extended reality (XR) environments by enabling gesture-based control through neural interface technology. It complements augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR) devices like Meta’s Orion AR glasses, offering expanded control beyond typical viewing limitations.

“As a CES 2025 Innovation Awards Honoree, Mudra Link reaffirms the demand for neural control wearables that enhance user experience seamlessly across platforms,” said Wearable Devices CEO Asher Dahan. The award showcases Mudra Link’s role in driving intuitive digital interaction, creating new possibilities in XR and AR spaces.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Wearable Devices' Mudra Link wristband earns CES 2025 Innovation Award for pioneering neural gesture control technology, advancing XR and AR user experiences.

item.title: Glowtape Wearable Display Lights Up Supercon with Innovation
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Glowtape Wearable Display Steals the Show at Supercon

The Glowtape wearable display, created by [Henner Zeller], dazzled attendees at Supercon 2024 with its innovative use of UV LEDs and glow-in-the-dark material. This unique timepiece doubles as an artistic canvas, displaying time, text, and images on a glowing strip of material.

How Glowtape Works

Glowtape uses a dense array of UV LEDs to energize glow-in-the-dark tape, producing bright and ephemeral displays of the time, date, or personalized messages. It offers a mesmerizing viewing experience, especially in low-light environments, as seen during Supercon’s evening events.

  • Features:
    • Displays time, date, and customizable text or images.
    • Fades quickly but refreshes dynamically, ideal for practical use.
    • Powered by an RP2040 microcontroller and housed in a 3D-printed enclosure.
  • Challenges:
    While captivating, the Glowtape requires managing a meter-long strip of material, a trade-off for its striking visuals.

Make It Yourself

For the DIY enthusiasts, [Henner] has shared the full source code and design files for Glowtape. From OpenSCAD scripts for 3D printing to C firmware for the microcontroller, everything you need to recreate this wearable marvel is available.

Beyond the Watch: Glowxels

The Glowtape’s LED array design is a spin-off of the Glowxels project, which applies this glowing display technology on a larger scale. It’s a testament to the versatility of UV-activated materials in creative projects.

Glowtape is a perfect blend of functionality and artistic expression, setting a new benchmark for innovative wearable displays.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover Glowtape, a wearable UV LED-powered display spotted at Supercon 2024. It features glowing time, text, and images in a compact, DIY-friendly design.

item.title: Can Wearable Technology Revolutionize Healthcare in India?
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Wearable devices are emerging as transformative tools in healthcare, offering early detection and continuous monitoring for various health conditions. However, integrating them into India's healthcare system requires addressing limitations in accuracy, accessibility, and data security.

Promise of Early Detection and Monitoring

Wearable devices, such as ECG trackers and respiratory monitors, hold immense potential for early detection of conditions like arrhythmias or sleep apnea. Experts emphasize their role in raising awareness rather than replacing clinical diagnostics.

  • Cardiac Monitoring:
    Wearable ECG and PPG devices can detect conditions like atrial fibrillation but may miss subtler issues. Extended Loop Recorders (ELRs) remain more precise for continuous monitoring.
  • Respiratory Health:
    Devices with biosensors and pulse oximetry can track metrics like VO2 max and SpO2 but face challenges in accuracy due to variables like movement or environmental factors.

Neurological and AI Integration

Wearables now track sleep patterns, stress levels, and motor functions, potentially detecting early signs of neurological disorders. Artificial intelligence amplifies their effectiveness by refining data analysis and reducing noise.

Challenges and Innovations

  1. Data Security:
    As devices collect sensitive health data, ensuring encryption and compliance with data protection norms like HIPAA is critical.
  2. Sensor Technology:
    Innovations like micro-nano fiber sensors are improving wearable accuracy, allowing detection of subtle pressure and temperature changes.
  3. Affordability and Accessibility:
    Collaboration with healthcare professionals to design cost-effective, reliable devices is essential for adoption in India.

The Future of Wearables in India

Wearable devices cannot replace clinical tools but are valuable for preventive care and real-time monitoring. Future advancements may enable wearables to measure lung function, respiratory distress markers, and more, making healthcare more accessible and efficient in India.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Explore how wearable devices are reshaping healthcare in India by enabling early detection, remote monitoring, and improved health management, along with their challenges.

item.title: Revolutionary Wearable Ultrasound Sensor Sets New Standard for Blood Pressure Monitoring
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Wearable Ultrasound Sensor Transforms Blood Pressure Monitoring

A novel wearable ultrasound sensor has been validated for its ability to provide continuous, accurate blood pressure (BP) readings across diverse clinical settings. Published in Nature Biomedical Engineering, the study highlights the device’s innovative design and practical applications, promising a significant leap forward in non-invasive BP monitoring.

A Game-Changer for BP Monitoring

Traditional methods like sphygmomanometers and invasive arterial lines have limitations in precision and usability. The newly developed wearable ultrasound sensor addresses these issues with:

  • Non-Invasive Design: Tracks BP without the discomfort or risks of invasive methods.
  • Continuous Monitoring: Captures fluctuations throughout the day, enabling early detection of conditions like paroxysmal hypertension and nocturnal hypertension.
  • Unmatched Durability: Maintains calibration for up to a year and withstands diverse conditions, including daily activities and clinical settings.

Innovative Features

  • Advanced Transducer: A redesigned sensor array ensures precise readings by minimizing vibrations and eliminating alignment errors.
  • Versatility: Suitable for use in homes, outpatient clinics, and intensive care units (ICUs).
  • Safety First: Complies with FDA safety recommendations, with no adverse effects from prolonged use.

Clinical Validation

The sensor showed exceptional accuracy, matching results from sphygmomanometers and invasive arterial lines with over 93% concordance in various settings. It was particularly effective in tracking BP changes during posture shifts, exercise, and ICU monitoring.

Looking Ahead

While the wearable sensor marks a breakthrough, additional studies are required to address challenges such as:

  • Recalibration Needs: For patients with severe hemodynamic instability.
  • Integration: Automated data transfer to hospital systems.
  • Power Efficiency: Enhancements for prolonged use.

This ultrasound BP sensor stands as a beacon of innovation, poised to revolutionize how we monitor and manage blood pressure in both clinical and at-home settings.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the groundbreaking wearable ultrasound sensor offering non-invasive, continuous blood pressure monitoring with unparalleled accuracy and convenience.

item.title: Hyundai and Kia Launch X-ble Shoulder Wearable Robot for Industry
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Hyundai and Kia Introduce X-ble Shoulder Wearable Robot

Hyundai Motor Company and Kia Corporation have unveiled the X-ble Shoulder, a lightweight wearable robot aimed at revolutionizing industrial work. Designed to enhance productivity and reduce musculoskeletal injuries, this innovation supports upper arm strength during overhead tasks.

X-ble Shoulder Features and Benefits

The X-ble Shoulder, revealed at the Wearable Robot Tech Day near Seoul, is part of Hyundai and Kia's X-ble line of wearable robotics.

  • Lightweight Design: Weighs just 1.9 kg, with an adjustable length (406-446 mm).
  • Material Strength: Built with carbon composites and wear-resistant materials for durability.
  • Muscle Strain Reduction: Reduces shoulder load by up to 60% and deltoid muscle activity by up to 30%.
  • Applications: Useful across sectors like construction, shipbuilding, aviation, agriculture, and automotive industries.

Future X-ble Innovations

Hyundai and Kia are expanding the X-ble lineup to include:

  • X-ble Waist: Assists with heavy lifting.
  • X-ble MEX: A medical robot supporting walking rehabilitation.

Dong Jin Hyun, Vice President and Head of Robotics LAB at Hyundai and Kia, stated that user feedback played a crucial role in developing the X-ble Shoulder.

Global Expansion and Market Potential

Initially available in Korea, Hyundai and Kia plan to launch the X-ble Shoulder in Europe and North America by 2026. They aim to meet the increasing demand in the wearable robotics market, projected to grow from $2.55 billion in 2024 to $10.25 billion by 2029 at a CAGR of 32.05%.

Implications for Industry and Beyond

Beyond industrial use, the X-ble Shoulder offers potential for healthcare, small businesses, and domestic tasks. It could assist physical therapists, aid home healthcare workers, and simplify household maintenance.

Competitors in Wearable Robotics

The X-ble Shoulder faces competition from:

  1. SuitX ShoulderX: Reduces muscle strain by up to 80%.
  2. Ottobock Paexo Shoulder: Lightweight and minimizes strain by 75%.
  3. ATOUN MODEL Y: Focuses on overhead and lifting tasks.

With its innovative features and broad applications, the X-ble Shoulder positions Hyundai and Kia as leaders in the growing wearable robotics sector.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Hyundai and Kia unveil the X-ble Shoulder wearable robot to enhance worker efficiency, reduce injuries, and support overhead tasks across industries.

item.title: Manipur Extends Mobile Internet Suspension in Nine Districts
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Imphal, Nov 29: In a preventive move, the Manipur government has extended the suspension of mobile internet and data services in nine districts, including the troubled Jiribam district, for two more days. According to a Home Department order, the ban, now effective until 5:15 p.m. on December 1, aims to prevent the misuse of social media for spreading hate speech and inciting unrest.

Precautionary Measures Amid Law and Order Concerns

The suspension order was issued by Commissioner (Home) N. Ashok Kumar, citing the potential for anti-social elements to exploit social media to disrupt peace. While no major incidents have been reported since November 18, the precaution follows recent unrest, including violence on November 16 triggered by the discovery of six bodies in Jiribam.

The affected districts are:

  • Imphal West
  • Imphal East
  • Bishnupur
  • Thoubal
  • Kakching
  • Kangpokpi
  • Churachandpur
  • Jiribam
  • Pherzawl

Impact on Daily Life and Education

The government has eased curfew restrictions in six districts to allow students to attend schools and universities, which resumed regular classes on Friday after a two-week hiatus. While attendance remained slightly low, it is expected to normalize soon.

Curfew relaxations also permit residents to procure essentials like food and medicines. However, gatherings and rallies still require prior approval from authorities.

Recurring Suspensions and Gradual Easing

Manipur has faced repeated mobile internet suspensions since November 16 following violent outbreaks. Despite the ongoing ban, efforts to restore normalcy continue, with educational institutions reopening and curfew restrictions gradually easing.

item.title: Why Doctors Remain Skeptical About Wearable Health Tech
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Wearable health technology, including devices like smartwatches and rings, has become a billion-dollar industry. These gadgets promise to monitor various health metrics, from heart rate to sleep patterns. Despite their popularity and potential, many doctors remain skeptical about their widespread integration into healthcare systems.

Promises of Wearable Health Tech

Wearables like the Apple Watch and Oura Ring claim to detect early health changes, such as elevated heart rates or temperature, and encourage users to adopt healthier lifestyles. Governments, including the UK's NHS, are considering providing wearables to patients to monitor health conditions remotely.

Some success stories highlight their potential. For example, wearables have flagged early signs of illness and even saved lives by detecting heart irregularities. Yet, many healthcare professionals approach these claims with caution.

Why Doctors Are Wary

1. Inaccuracy and False Positives

Dr. Helen Salisbury, a GP, notes that wearables often generate false positives due to minor blips or device errors. These inaccuracies can lead to unnecessary stress or hypochondria. She emphasizes that while wearables may encourage better habits, their data isn’t always reliable enough for medical diagnosis.

2. Limited Clinical Usefulness

Wearables often lack the precision of medical-grade devices. Dr. Yang Wei from Nottingham Trent University points out that wearables must balance accuracy with practical limitations like battery life and portability. For example, measuring heart activity from the finger sacrifices accuracy compared to wrist or chest-based readings.

3. Standardization Issues

There is no global standard for wearable data collection, making it challenging for clinicians to trust or integrate wearable data into healthcare systems. In many cases, doctors prefer to confirm findings using their own equipment.

Potential Challenges in Integration

  • Overburdening Healthcare Systems: Excessive reliance on wearables may lead to more patients seeking consultations for minor alerts, overwhelming healthcare providers.
  • Infrastructure Gaps: The UK's healthcare system lacks the infrastructure to seamlessly integrate wearable data, according to Pritesh Mistry from the Kings Fund.
  • User Behavior: Wearable accuracy depends on consistent usage, which isn’t always practical for all users.

Real-World Issues

Wearables have caused unnecessary emergency alerts. For instance, one Apple Watch mistakenly flagged a man’s high-speed driving as a car crash, sending an alert to his emergency contact. Such incidents highlight the need for better alert thresholds and system improvements.

The Verdict

Wearables hold promise for promoting healthier lifestyles and early health monitoring. However, their integration into mainstream healthcare faces significant challenges, including accuracy, standardization, and system readiness.

Doctors advocate using wearables as supplementary tools rather than replacements for medical expertise, reinforcing the timeless advice: eat healthily, exercise regularly, and maintain a balanced lifestyle.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Explore why doctors are cautious about wearable health tech like smartwatches. Learn about accuracy issues, false positives, and integration challenges in healthcare.

item.title: Stretchable Gel-Based Nanogenerators Are Going to Revolutionize Wearables
item.dc:creator: divyanshsharma7117
item.content:encoded:
Introduction

Wearable devices like fitness trackers and medical sensors are changing how we interact with technology. A new breakthrough by engineers at Dongguk University introduces gel-based triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs) that promise to power these devices sustainably, eliminating the need for traditional batteries.

The Innovation: Gel Polymer Electrode-Based TENGs

To address challenges in flexibility and durability, Professor Jung Inn Sohn's team developed a gel polymer electrode-based triboelectric nanogenerator (GPE-TENG).

The device:

  • Flexible and durable: Stretches up to 375% without damage.
  • Semi-transparent: Ideal for discreet wearable applications.
  • Long-lasting performance: Withstands two months of continuous use without degradation.

How It Works

The team utilized an innovative in-situ curing process to create the GPE-TENG:

  1. Materials Used: A gel mix of polyethylene oxide (PEO) and lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide (LiTFSI).
  2. Fabrication Process:
    • Gel poured into an ecoflex mold.
    • Covered with another ecoflex layer.
    • Cured at 70°C for 12 hours to ensure strong bonding.
  3. Output: Generates 0.36 W/m² peak power under a 15 MΩ load.

Applications in Wearable Technology

The GPE-TENG has diverse potential uses, including:

  • Health Monitoring: Tracking joint activity for rehabilitation.
  • Biometric Systems: Enabling smart door or locker access.
  • Sustainable Power: Powering wearable sensors without batteries.

"This technology paves the way for flexible and sustainable electronics, with applications in health care, security, and biometrics," says Prof. Sohn.

Conclusion

The gel-based TENG developed by Dongguk University marks a significant step forward in wearable technology. Its flexibility, durability, and sustainable energy output make it a promising solution for next-generation wearables.
